cards.passage:
W: Enrich supplies. How may I help you?
M: Hi, I'd like to place an order.
W: Your account number please.
M: MM444861.
W: Delta Express?
M: Yes.
W: OK. Go ahead.
M: I need 16 washroom signs. 8 men's and 8 women's. Item number 442 in your catalogue. I also need 6 handicapped signs. Item 612. And last, I need 12 custom signs. Item 102.
W: What would you like printed on those?
M: They are all employee names. But I'd like to make sure the spelling is correct on all of them. Could I email them to you?
W: Sure, that would be fine.
cards.passageTranslation:
W：豐漾辦公用品。有甚麼我可以幫忙的？
M：嗨，我想下一份訂單。
W：請說你的帳號編號。
M：MM444861。
W：達美快遞嗎？
M：是的。
W：OK。請繼續說。
M：我需要16張洗手間的標誌牌。八張男士的，八張女士的。你的目錄中商品編號442號。我也需要6張身障用的。目錄編號612。我需要12張可自訂的，編號102。
W：你想要印製甚麼？
M：都是職員的名字。但我想要確認姓名的拼字都正確。我可以email給你嗎？
W：好的，沒有問題。
MM444861 訂單
商品編號 數量 88 16 102 12 442 16 612 6

questions.stem: What kind of product is being ordered?
translation: 訂單中訂購的商品為以下何種？
a. 浴廁用品。
b. 紙本表格。
c. 招牌。
d. 文具店。

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. Which item on this table was not ordered by the man?
translation: 請見圖表。表格中哪一項商品不是該男子訂購的？

questions.stem: Why does the man want to email the woman?
translation: 該男子為什麼要寄送email給該女子？
a. 再次確認訂單。
b. 確認名字拼字都正確。
c. 知會她一些員工問題。
d. 要求一份客製的訂單。
options: (B) To make sure the names are spelled correctly.
(C) To notify her about some employee problems.

cards.passage:
W: Jim, you know there's a dress code for the banquet this evening, right?
M: Of course I do. Don't worry. I have a change of clothes in the back room of the office. Do you really think I'd show up in this casual attire?
W: That's precisely what you did last time! Didn't you feel out of place?
M: To be honest, I couldn't have cared less. But I did hear about it from the boss afterwards. Hey, do you know if we are allowed to invite a guest? I'd like to bring this new girl I'm seeing.
W: Well, according to the official invitation, they said dates are allowed, but that you have to notify them in advance.
M: These company banquets always have a low turnout. Don't you remember how many seats were free last time? I think I'm going to just show up and see what happens. I can't imagine they would deny my guest entrance!
W: You really like to take chances with embarrassing yourself, don't you?
cards.passageTranslation:
女:吉姆，你知道今晚的宴會有服裝規定，對吧?
男:我當然知道。別擔心。我在辦公室後面房間裡有一套換洗衣服。你真以為我會以這身休閒裝扮出現嗎?
女:那正是你上回的樣子!你沒有覺得格格不入嗎?
男:老實說，我根本不在乎。但我事後有聽到老闆說起這件事。嘿，你知道我們是否可以攜伴參加嗎?我想要帶我正在交往的新女伴去。
女:嗯，依照這張正式的邀請函來看，他們說可以攜伴，但你必須先知會他們。
男:這些公司宴會總是出席率很低。你不記得上次有多少空位了嗎?我想我要直接出現看看會怎樣。我無法想像他們會拒絕讓我的女伴入場!
女:你很喜歡嘗試運氣讓自己難堪，對吧?
vocabulary: 1. banquet n. 宴會/ v.參加宴會
2. attire n. 服裝/ v. 裝飾； 打扮
5. in advance adv. 在前面;；預先
6. turnout n. 出席者；產量；裝備
Ex. I can't imagine what has happened.我想像不出發生了什麼事。
8. embarrass v. 使尷尬； 妨礙
9. focus n. 焦點；重點/ v.聚焦；集中
Ex. She always wants to be the center of attention.
她總是想成為人們注意的焦點。

questions.stem: How did the man feel when he showed up in casual clothing at the last company banquet?
translation: 這個男人穿著休閒服出現在公司上次的晚宴時有何感覺?
a. 尷尬
b. 困窘的
c. 他不在乎
d. 他沒有注意到，因為他集中注意力在她女朋友身上
options: (D) He didn't notice, because he was focused on his girlfriend.

questions.stem: Why is he so sure that his date will get in?
translation: 為什麼他這麼確定她可以攜伴參加?
a. 因為他事先通知他們了
b. 因為她已經有票了
c. 因為攜伴參加是被允許的
d. 因為應該有空位
options: (A) Because he notified them in advance.
(D) Because there should be free space.

questions.stem: How is Jim going to dress tonight?
translation: 吉姆今晚將穿什麼?
a. 他會穿上任何他的女伴喜歡的
b. 他將會休閒裝扮
c. 這次他可能會作正式穿著
d. 他還沒決定要穿什麼
options: (A) He will wear whatever his date likes on him.
(B) He will dress in casual attire.
(C) He will probably dress formally this time.

cards.passage:
W: Dr. Simmons, some of the customers have been complaining about the long waiting times recently.
W2: I agree. We are always more and more behind schedule as the day progresses.
M: Do you think that perhaps we should post a notice informing patients to expect delays?
W: Yes, I think that would be helpful.
W2: Could I also suggest that we provide some forms of entertainment to help waiting customers pass the time?
M: What a great idea. Like perhaps some magazines?
W2: Sure. If we could install a television as well, I feel the patients would especially appreciate that.
W: Yes, some of them are nervous for their appointments, so I think it would help to alleviate some stress.
M: I don't see why not.
W2: Oh, and one final idea. A lot of parents come in with children, who tend to get a little impatient. A few building blocks or children's books could make a really big difference.
M: Alright, Laura. Could I put you in charge of arranging these items? If you could do some online investigation, and order a few items, that would be much appreciated. Go ahead and bill it to the office account.
W: Sure thing, Doctor.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：西蒙醫生，最近有一些客人抱怨候診時間太久了。
女2：我同意。愈到下午時間我們的進度就愈落後。
男：你認為或許我們該貼張告示告訴病患們要有時間耽誤的心理準備嗎?
女：是的，我想那會有所幫助。
女2：我可否再建議我們提供某些型態的娛樂來幫助等候的客人打發時間呢?
男：真是個好主意。或許像是些雜誌嗎?
女2：可以。如果我們還可以裝部電視機的話，我覺得患者們會十分激賞的。
女：他們有些人會對約診感到緊張，所以我想這會有助於緩和些緊張。
男：有何不可。
女2：喔，還有最後一個主意。許多的小朋友和父母親一起來，往往會有些不耐煩。放一些積木或童書會讓情況大大的不同。
男：好吧，蘿拉。我能請你負責統籌這些品項嗎?如果你能在網路上做些調查再訂購些品項的話會令人十分感激的。儘管去做把帳單由診所來付。
女：好的，醫生。
vocabulary: Ex. I would suggest that he consult a lawyer. 我建議他請教律師。
4. perhaps adj. 或許/ n.假定
Ex. He's going to install an air conditioner in the house.
他要在這屋子裡裝冷氣機。
6. appreciate v. 欣賞；感謝；體會
Ex. I appreciate your frankness. 我欣賞你的坦率。
7. appointment n. (正式的) 約會；任命
9. impatient adj. 不耐煩的；切盼的

questions.stem: What is the job title of the two women?
translation: 以下選項何者是這兩位女士的工作頭銜？
a. 老師
b. 醫生
c. 牙醫
d. 秘書

questions.stem: Which of these recommended items is not proposed by either woman for the waiting area?
translation: 這些用於等候區的物件中，那一項兩位女士都沒提及？
a. 小孩的玩具
b. 雜誌
c. 給成人的娛興書籍
d. 一台電視

questions.stem: Why might some patients be nervous?
translation: 為什麼有一些病人可能會緊張?
a. 因為他們病的很重
b. 因為小孩子到處玩
c. 因為他們約診遲到了
d. 因為看牙醫可能會緊張
options: (B) Because the children are playing everywhere.
(C) Because they are late for their appointments.
(D) Because seeing a dentist can be stressful.

cards.passage:
M: Cindy, I want to congratulate you and your team on successfully meeting all sales targets in the last quarter. Not only did you guys meet them but you exceeded them by over 30%! We only have one question, though. I'm dying to know: what strategy did you use to push your team?
W: Well, I hate to give away my secrets, but since you insist, I'll let you in on it. I went with the oldest trick in the book. I made an internal competition of it, and allotted prizes to the top-three sellers on the team. A little incentive never hurts!
M: So what were the incentives, exactly?
W: What do most salespeople enjoy the most after a long day's work? A round of drinks, of course!
M: That's brilliant!
W: Better yet, the agreement was that those with the lowest figures had to buy all the rounds!
cards.passageTranslation:
男:辛蒂，我要恭喜你和你的小組圓滿達成上一季所有的銷售目標。你們不但是達成了目標，還超出了30%!但我們只有一個問題。我很想知道:你用甚麼策略來督促你的小組?
女:嗯，我不喜歡把我的秘訣送人，但既然你堅持，我就告訴你吧。我用書上的老招數。我做了一個內部銷售競爭，把獎品分給小組裏的銷售前三名。一點小誘因是無傷大雅的!
男:所以這誘因到底是甚麼?
女:甚麼是大部分的業務員在一整天漫長的工作後最喜歡的?當然是請大家喝上一輪酒囉!
男:這真是高明!
女:還有更棒的，協議是銷售成績最低的那些人要負責買單!
vocabulary: 1. quarter n.四分之一；季/ v.把…分四等分；住宿
Ex. The sales exceeded my expectations. 銷售超過了我的預料。
3. secret adj. 祕密的/ n.祕密
4. trick n.詭計 v. 哄騙；惡作劇
Ex. Each speaker was allotted five minutes.
每個發言者限定發言五分鐘。
7. incentive n. 刺激；鼓勵/ a. 刺激的；鼓勵的
Ex. The child has no incentive to study harder because his parents cannot afford to send him to college.這孩子沒有努力學習的動力,因為他父母供不起他上大學。
8. result v. 產生；結果/ n.結果；決定
9. expression n. 表達；表情；詞句
10. effective adj. 有效的；給人深刻印象的

questions.stem: What was the woman's 'secret incentive'?
translation: 這個女人的秘密誘因是什麼?
a. 她請銷售高手們喝酒
b. 銷售成績最低的必須要請銷售成績最高的喝酒
c. 她請銷售高手們到酒吧喝上一輪酒
d. 他們有喝酒的競賽
options: (A) She bought drinks for the top sellers.
(B) The lowest sellers had to treat the highest sellers.
(C) She took the top sellers round to a bar.

questions.stem: What were the results of her strategy?
translation: 她的策略成效如何?
a. 他們只低於銷售目標
b. 他們達到所有的目標
c. 他們超越了目標
d. 他們修改了所有的目標

questions.stem: What does the woman mean by the expression, 'the oldest trick in the book'?
translation: 這個女人所說的"書上用的老招數"是指什麼?
a. 一個慣用且有效的策略
b. 一個老年人慣用於年輕人身上的招數
c. 一個出自一本非常有名的書上的招數
d. 一個出自基督教聖書上的策略
options: (A) A commonly used and effective strategy.
(B) A common way for old people to trick young people.
(C) A trick from a very famous book.
(D) A strategy that originates in the Christian holy book.

cards.passage:
W: Hello. Is this the Sam Evans who filed the official complaint to the City of Edmonton waste management department on November 12th of this year?
M: Indeed, that's who you're speaking with.
W: We are calling to notify you that your complaint has been taken seriously. We understand that the weekly trash disposal teams have not been adequately clearing the waste from the large dumpster located in the rear parking lot of your business. Is this correct?
M: Precisely.
W: Has the situation improved since you made the complaint?
M: Actually, no, it has not. Furthermore, I would like to add to it. Not only are the garbage men failing to remove all the contents of the dumpster, but also recently I have noticed that they like to take smoke breaks in my company's parking area. In fact, some of my customers use the rear entrance to our building, so this can be bothersome to them.
W: Okay. I have taken note of everything that you have just told me. I am going to forward this information directly to the manager of the disposal units. If the problem still persists next week, I'd like you to call me immediately.
cards.passageTranslation:
女:哈囉。請問您是在十一月12日向艾德蒙頓市廢棄物管理局提出正式投訴函的山姆艾凡斯嗎?
男:沒錯，和你說話的就是。
女:我們來電是要通知你您的投訴函十分受到重視。我們了解的是每周的垃圾處理隊並沒能自您公司後方停車場的大型垃圾箱充分的清除垃圾。是這樣嗎?
男:完全正確。
女:自您投訴後情況有改善嗎?
男:事實上，沒有，情況沒有改善。而且，我還要作補充。垃圾清潔員不只是沒有移除垃圾箱裡的內容物，而且我最近還注意到他們喜歡在我公司的停車場抽菸小憩。事實上，我的一些顧客使用後方入口進入我們的建築物，所以對他們頗為困擾。
女:好的。我已經把您剛向我說的每件事全記下來。我會把這資訊直接轉遞給清運單位的經理。如果這問題到下禮拜仍持續著，我想請您立即打電話給我。
vocabulary: 1. official adj.官方的；公務上的/ n. 官員
4. dumpster n.大型垃圾裝卸卡車
5. rear n.後面；臀部adj. 後面的
6. remove v.移動；去掉；遷移; n.距離；遷移
7. bothersome adj.令人討厭的；麻煩的
Ex. The storm persisted for a week. 暴風雨持續了一個星期。
9. block v.阻塞;限制; adj. 大塊的；交通堵塞的/ n.塊； 阻塞(物)
Ex. A piece of rock had fallen and blocked the path.
一塊岩石掉了下來，堵住了路。
10. responsible adj. 需負責任的；有責任感的

questions.stem: What new complaint does the man make during this phone call?
translation: 在電話中這個男人有什麼新的抱怨?
a. 垃圾清潔員沒有拿走所有的垃圾
b. 垃圾清潔員擋住他做生意的出入口
c. 垃圾清潔員弄得亂七八糟
d. 垃圾清潔員在後方入口抽菸
options: (A) The garbage men are not taking all the garbage.
(B) The garbage men are blocking the entrance to his business.
(C) The garbage men are making a mess.
(D) The garbage men are smoking near the rear entrance.

questions.stem: What is the woman going to do after this phone call?
translation: 打完這通電話之後這個女人將會怎麼做?
a. 告訴清潔隊員把工作做好一點
b. 把垃圾箱立刻移走
c. 把這個客訴轉給負責的經理
d. 把需要負責的工人開除
options: (A) Tell the waste disposal workers to do a better job.
(C) Pass the complaint on to the appropriate manager.

questions.stem: What was the effect of the man's original complaint on November 12th?
translation: 這個男人在十一月十二日的初次客訴有什麼效果?
a. 問題沒有改善
b. 工作人員停止收他的垃圾
c. 工作人員開始在那裡抽菸
d. 工作人員解決了這個問題
options: (B) The workers stopped taking his garbage.
(C) The workers started smoking there too.

cards.passage:
M: Teresa, after meeting with the GM, we have decided to transfer you to the printer team.
W: But I was just getting used to the PC team and building up a network of clients. This is the third time!
M: We realize it may inconvenience you in the short term. But we've been following your progress carefully since you first transferred out of the surveillance camera unit, and we have finally come to be certain that your home is the printer team. Trust me, this will be the best for you in the long run.
W: I guess I don't have much of a choice. Can you promise me this will be the last time?
M: I'd like to, but the future is always unpredictable, so I can't make such a promise.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：Teresa，與總經理開會後，我們決定把你轉到印表機團隊。
女：但我才剛習慣PC電腦團隊且與建立好客戶網絡。這是第三次了！
男：我們意識到在短期內可能會造成不便。但是，打從我們把你調出監控攝像單位就一直仔細地關注你的進展，我們也最終可以肯定，印表機團隊是你最後的歸宿。相信我，這將是最適合你的長遠目標。
女：我想我沒有多少選擇的餘地。你能答應我這是最後一次嗎？
男：我希望我可以，但未來永遠是不可預知的，所以我不能做這樣的承諾。
HTS電腦
部門列表
1. 監控攝像部門
2. 印表機部門
3. PC電腦部門
4. 軟體部門

questions.stem: What do we know about Teresa's position in this company?
translation: 以下關於Teresa在這家公司的職位何者正確？
a. 職位變更了好幾次。
b. 她很快會被升職。
c. 她從基層做起並一路向上爬。
d. 這是一個兼職職位。
options: (C) She started at the bottom and worked her way up.

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. Which department has Teresa never worked in or won't work in in the near future?
translation: 請看圖表。以下何者是Teresa從未工作過或不久的將來也不會在那裡工作的部門？

questions.stem: What does the man imply at the end?
translation: 該男士在結尾時所說的話暗指為何？
a. 如果Teresa再抱怨可能會失去她的工作。
b. Teresa 在未來可能會再次轉調部門
c. 他暗示她可能不喜歡她的新崗位。
d. 他暗示這個決定並非由他做主。
options: (A) Teresa may lose her job if she complains again.
(B) Teresa could be transferred again in the future.
(C) He implies that she won't like her new role.
(D) He implies that this decision wasn't made by him.

cards.passage:
W: What exactly is required to rent one of these scooters overnight?
M: First of all, do you have a driver's license and proof that you are over 18?
W: I don't, but my husband does.
M2: That's right. Is an international license acceptable?
M: Yes, that will suffice, but only you will be able to drive, sir. Secondly, you'll have to sign this waiver form, which states that you are to be held responsible for any damage caused the scooter during your rental. Oh, and we also ask that you leave a 100 dollar deposit or any major credit card.
W: I don't have either of those on me at the moment.
M2: Could I leave my health insurance card or passport?
M: Either of those would suffice.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：要租一台這種摩托車隔夜確切需要甚麼東西？
男：首先，妳有駕照而並可證明妳超過18歲嗎？
女：我沒有。但我丈夫有嗎？
男2：那可以。國際駕照可以嗎？
男：那就夠了，但只有你才可以開車，先生。第二，你得簽這張同意書，聲明在你的租賃期間你必須對造成摩托車的任何損壞負責。喔，而且我們還要求你留下100美金的押金或是任何大公司的信用卡。
女：我現在兩種都沒有帶在身上。
男2：我能留我的健康保險卡或護照嗎？
男：任何一個就夠了。
vocabulary: 1. exactly adv. 確切地；恰好地
3. proof n. 證據/ v. 校對
6. deposit v. 付(保證金) / n . 存款；押金
8. second-hand adj. 二手的；中古的
Ex. One of her eccentricities is sleeping under the bed instead of on it.
她的怪癖之一是睡在床底下而不睡在床上。
Ex. His family possessed a large fortune.
他的家族擁有大筆財富。

questions.stem: What is the couple trying to do during their visit?
translation: 以下選項是這對夫妻在這段期間要做什麼？
a. 租摩托車一下午。
b. 租摩托車幾天。
c. 買一部二手摩托車。
d. 買一部不須訂金的摩托車。
options: (A) Rent a scooter for the afternoon.
(B) Rent a scooter for a couple days.
(D) Purchase a scooter without a deposit.

questions.stem: Which of the following can the man leave instead of 100$?
translation: 以下選項何者是這位先生可以留下來代替美金100元？
a. 大公司的信用卡。
b. 駕照。
c. 證明他已滿18歲的身份證。
d. 他的健康保險卡。
options: (C) ID that show he is over 18.

questions.stem: What kind of driver's license does the man possess?
translation: 以下選項何者是這位男子持有的駕照？
a. 當地駕照。
b. 國際駕照。
c. 健康駕照。
d. 一個不同國家的駕照。
options: (D) A license from a different country.

cards.passage:
M: Are there any withdrawal fees associated with this debit card?
W: So long as you use a Toronto Trust ATM, then all withdrawals from your account are 100% free of charge. The same applies if you withdraw cash in person at any of our branches.
M: What about if I withdraw from other ATMs or internationally?
W: There is a flat rate fee of 50 cents per transaction, no matter the amount, from any non-Toronto Trust ATM, plus any additional fees added by the bank, which may range from nothing to 2.50. For international withdrawals, it is a flat rate of 5 dollars per transaction, plus additional fees imposed by the local bank.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：這張現金卡有任何提款手續費嗎？
女：只要你是使用多倫多信託的自動提款機，那麼所有從你帳戶的提款都百分之百免費。如果你親自在任何一家分行提現金也是如此。
男：那如果我從其他的自動提款機或跨國提款呢？
女：從任何非多倫多信託的自動提款機的每筆交易固定手續費是五十分，不論金額，還有銀行加收的附加費，幅度從零到2.5。至於跨國提款。每筆交易的固定費用是五美金，加上當地銀行所加收的費用。
vocabulary: 1. withdraw v. 提取；收回；取消
Ex. You can't withdraw money from this ATM because it's not your bank.你不能從這台提款機提款因為這間不是發卡銀行。
3. debit card (銀行存款戶持有的)信用卡
4. account n. 帳戶；解釋/ v. 解釋；導致
7. range n. 幅度；排；級別/ v. 排列；分類
8. impose v. 徵(稅) ；利用；欺騙
Ex. The government imposes a heavy tax on luxury goods.
政府對奢侈品課以重稅。

questions.stem: What is the man most likely doing today?
translation: 該男子今天最可能要做什麼？
a. 簽約貸款。
b. 付清信用卡帳單。
c. 嘗試使用自動提款機。
d. 申辦新的現金卡。
options: (B) Paying off his credit card bills.
(D) Applying for a new debit card.

questions.stem: How much of a fee is applied for a withdrawal from a Toronto Trust ATM?
translation: 從多倫多信託自動提款機提款的手續費是多少？
a. 免費。
b. 五十分。
c. 五十分再加上附加手續費。
d. 2.50 再加上附加手續費。

questions.stem: What's the main difference between taking money from a non-Toronto Trust bank inside of and outside of the country?
translation: 在國內與國外的非多倫多信託銀行提款時最大的差異為何？
a. 國外較便宜。
b. 國外固定費較低。
c. 國外固定費較高
d. 國外附加費用較低。
options: (B) Outside has a lower flat rate.
(C) Outside has a higher flat rate.

cards.passage:
W: What do you think we should give away as prizes at the upcoming year-end party?
M: The flat screen televisions were a big hit at last year's party.
W: Do you know why?
M: I assumed the employees just wanted them.
W: No. Do you remember Jessie from R&D won the biggest one? She told me that she later sold it online for three thousand dollars.
M: Wow! So I guess they just want money.
W: That's what I was thinking. Why don't we just give cash prizes?
M: I'll run it by Mr. Reinhardt and see what he thinks. I know he is big on gift giving, but we'll see.
W: Sure, and just in case he says no, I'd like you to prepare a list of alternative ideas.
M: Will do.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：你認為在即將到來的年終晚會我們應該送什麼獎品？
男：平板電視在去年的晚會大受歡迎。
女：你知道為什麼嗎？
男：我認為員工就是想要那個。
女：不，你記得研發部的Jessie?得頭獎嗎？她告訴我她後來在網路上賣了三千美元。
男：哇！所以我猜他們只是想要錢。
女：這就是我的想法。我們為什麼不乾脆給現金大獎？
男：我會告知Reinhardt先生看他怎麼想。我知道他喜歡送禮品， 就看他怎麼決定吧。
女：好，以防萬一他不贊同，我要你準備一份替代的清單。
男：知道了。
年終晚會潛在獎品清單
去年的獎品：
平板電視
平板
USB記憶卡
今年的晚會點子：
現金？
公司自助餐廳免費餐卷
包餐包宿家庭假期
百貨公司禮物卡
威士忌或葡萄酒

questions.stem: What are employees in this company doing with prizes that they win at the year-end party?
translation: 以下選項何者是這家公司的員工對年終晚會的得獎品想做的事？
a. 為了獎品爭吵。
b. 忘了獎品的存在。
c. 不關心獎品。
d. 賣掉獎品。

questions.stem: Why might Mr. Reinhardt not like the idea of giving cash prizes?
translation: 為什麼Reinhardt先生可能會不喜歡發現金當獎品的想法？
a. 他很小氣。
b. 該公司沒有大筆的預算可用在這活動上。
c. 他習慣送禮品。
d. 他很固執。
options: (B) The company doesn't have a big budget for this event.
(C) He is a fan of the practice of giving gifts.

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. Who wrote this?
translation: 請看圖表。這是誰寫的？
a. 對話中的男子
b. 對話中的女子
c. 研發部的Jessie
d. Reinhardt先生

cards.passage:
W: Hello. I'd like to speak with the store manager please.
M: I'm sorry. She's not available at the moment. I'm the assistant manager. Is there any way that I can be of assistance to you today?
W: I guess so. I would like to file a complaint.
M: Certainly. We are very concerned if you had a poor shopping experience.
W: Okay. So I was in your store browsing the young adult titles. I was a little short of time, and needed some recommendations for my teenage son who has a mild reading disability. It was impossible to find any staff in your shop to assist me. All clerks at the till were occupied chatting with each other, so I tried one of the computer catalogues, but the system seemed to be down.
M: I'm very sorry to hear about your experience. Could you tell me the time of your visit so I can find out which employees were responsible?
cards.passageTranslation:
女：你好。麻煩你我要找店經理。
男：我很抱歉，她目前不在。我是助理經理，今天有甚麼是我可以為您效勞的嗎？
女：我想有。我想要提出客訴。
男：當然沒問題。我們非常在意您是否有不佳的購物經驗。
女：好吧。我當時正在你們店裡瀏覽青少年商品。我有點趕時間，而且需要一些針對我的患有中度閱讀障礙的十幾歲兒子的購物建議。在你們店裡完全找不到任何一位店員可以幫我。在收銀台的店員全部只顧著相互聊天，我只好試了其中一個電腦目錄，可是系統似乎是壞了。
男：聽到您的遭遇我感到很抱歉。能請您告訴我您來店的時間我好查出是那些員工該負責嗎？
vocabulary: 1. assistant n. 助理；adj. 助理的；有幫助的
2. complaint n. 抱怨；控訴 complain about…v. 對…抱怨
3. concern v. 關心；涉及/ n. 關係；關心的事
安德魯表示了他的關切。
5. catalogue n. 目錄；登記/v. 為...編目；登記
Ex. The manager decided to purchase a new computer system.
該經理決定購買新式電腦系統。
7. employee n. 雇員 *employer n.雇主

questions.stem: What is the man's job title?
translation: 該男子的職稱為何？
a. 書店經理。
b. 書店助理經理。
c. 書店店員。
d. 書店客服代表。

questions.stem: What happened when the woman tried to look up a book on the computer catalogue?
translation: 該女子嘗試在電腦上搜尋一本書的時候發生了什麼事？
a. 她找不到那部電腦。
b. 她在目錄裡找不到那本書。
c. 職員不知道如何使用它。
d. 目錄無法使用。
options: (B) She couldn't find the book in the catalogue.
(C) The staff didn't know how to use it.

questions.stem: Why does the man want to know the time of her visit?
translation: 為何該男子想知道她來訪的時間？
a. 為了找出當時是誰當班。
b. 為了查明是否他該負責。
c. 為了研判為何該系統當時無法使用。
d. 為了確認那時是否真的有營業。
options: (A) To figure out who was working.
(B) To see if he was responsible.
(C) To determine why the system was down at that time.
(D) To check if the store was really open then.

cards.passage:
W: Hello. Is Mr. Stanford available?
M: Speaking.
W: This Alayna O'Riley calling from Dr. Stevenson's Dental & Orthodontist Clinic. I'd like to reconfirm your appointment to have your braces refitted tomorrow afternoon at 9 in the morning.
M: Darn! The appointment totally slipped my mind! I've scheduled a really important meeting that I can't miss. Is there any way we could reschedule to a later time?
W: I don't have any openings until the following afternoon, say, at 2:30?
M : That doesn't work for me either. Is there anything after 5pm on that day?
W: Currently, the early evening is fully booked, but how about I pencil you in for 5pm or later, and if there are any cancellations, I'll give you a call.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：哈囉。請問史丹福先生在嗎？
男：我就是。
女：我是史帝文生醫生牙齒矯正所的愛蕾娜歐萊利。我想要再次確認你明天上午九點要重新安裝牙套的預約。
男：糟了！我完全忘了有預約這回事！我已經約好一個十分重要我不能缺席的會議。有任何可能重新安排到一個晚一點的時間嗎？
女：直到隔天下午前我沒有任何空檔，那麼2點半如何？
男：那我也沒辦法。當天下午五點以後呢？
女：目前傍晚都約滿了，不然我用鉛筆把你寫在下午五點或晚一點，如果有任何人取消我會打電話給你。
vocabulary: Ex. I've made an appointment with Mr. Smith.
我已與史密斯先生有約會。
2. orthodontist n. 牙齒矯正醫師
4. braces n. 矯正器；支撐物
Ex. I will have my braces taken off before my wedding. 婚禮前我會拆牙套。

questions.stem: What happened to the man's scheduled appointment for tomorrow?
translation: 這名男子明天安排好的預約怎麼了？
a. 牙齒矯正診所改了時間。
b. 他完全忘了這件事。
c. 他因為有約會不能去。
d. 那女子告訴他時間不允許。
options: (C) He can't make it because of a date.
(D) The woman told him the time's not available.

questions.stem: Will the man be able to get his braces fitted the next day?
translation: 那男子隔天能夠裝他的牙套嗎？
a. 是的，一定可以。
b. 不行，一定不可以。
c. 除非有任何人取消。
d. 除非他可以錯過他的會議。
options: (D) Only if he can miss his meeting.

questions.stem: Why did she use a pencil to write his new time?
translation: 為什麼她用鉛筆寫下他的新時間？
a. 因為她沒有鋼筆。
b. 因為那還不確定而且容易擦掉。
c. 因為他們一直是用鉛筆預約。
a. 因為牙齒矯正醫師認為用鋼筆不健康。
options: (A) Because she doesn't have a pen.
(B) Because it's not certain and easy to erase.
(C) Because they always use a pencil for appointments.
(D) Because orthodontists think pens are unhealthy.

cards.passage:
M: Do you plan on visiting any other famous sights in the city, such as the Natural Museum, or the Gallery of Fine Arts?
W: Yes, I do. Why do you ask?
M: Well, your entrance ticket to the Museum of World Religions is 4.99. But for 8.99, you can buy a City Attractions Pass that is valid for over 10 tourist sights in the city, including the ones that I mentioned.
W: That sounds like a bargain. The problem is, my stopover in this city is for 3 days. I probably won't continue my sightseeing until tomorrow or the day after.
M: That's fine. The pass is valid for a week.
W: Excellent! I'll take it. Can you provide me with a list of all the locations that it is applicable at?
M: You can find that info on the back of the pass.
cards.passageTranslation:
會男：你是否計劃參觀該城市裏其他著名景點，像是自然博物館，或是美術館？
女：我是。你為什麼問？
男：嗯，你進世界宗教博物館的入場券是美金4.99元。可是花8.99元，你可以買一張可逛市內十個以上景點的城市觀光通行證，包括我提過的那個。
女：那聽起來很划算。問題是，我在這個城市會停留三天。我可能明天或後天才會再繼續遊覽。
男：沒關係。那本通行證可用一個禮拜。
女：太棒了！我要了。你可否幫我列出一張所有可以使用它的景點的清單？
男：你可以在這本通行證的背面找得到。
vocabulary: Ex. We are looking forward to their visit with eager anticipation.
我們殷切地期待著他們來訪。
3. sight n. 景色；觀光地；視力/ v. 看見
4. gallery n. 美術館；畫廊；走廊
Ex. According to the guidebook, there aren't many attractions in this city.
看看旅遊導覽，這城市沒有很多觀光景點。
6. pass n.通行證；經過/ v. 經過；傳遞

questions.stem: What tourist attraction is the woman visiting right now?
translation: 該女子現在正在參觀什麼旅遊景點？
a. 自然博物館。
b. 美術館。
c. 世界宗教博物館。
d. 最有名的城市景點。

questions.stem: How can she learn which other places the pass works at?
translation: 她如何得知哪些地方可以使用該通行證？
a. 該男子已經告訴她了。
b. 只有這個地方能用。
c. 有列在通行證上。
d. 她必須去那些地方才能察覺。
options: (B) It's only good at this location.
(D) She has to go to them to find out.

questions.stem: What does she think about the City Attractions Pass?
translation: 她覺得城市觀光通行證如何？
a. 不值得多花錢。
b. 很划算。
c. 使用容易。
d. 有點問題。
options: (A) It's not worth the extra money.

cards.passage:
M: I'm considering getting rid of all these old bookcases and shelves. What do you think about that?
W: Well, some of them are falling apart, so I think it would make this place look at lot nicer. Also, they don't match with the new paint job we did.
M: The only problem is, we don't really have the budget to replace them for now.
W: Have you heard of Furniture Depot? They sell discount furniture with monthly payment programs. I think the minimum per item is only 10 dollars a month.
M: That sounds perfect!
cards.passageTranslation:
男：我正在考慮丟掉全部這些舊書櫥跟架子。你認為如何？
女：嗯，它們有些快解體了，所以我覺得這樣應該會讓這個地方看起來好很多。而且，它們和我們剛漆上的新油漆不搭。
男：唯一的問題是，我們現在真的沒有更換它們的預算。
女：你有聽過傢具補給站嗎？他們賣的折扣傢具有月付分期方案。我想每一樣的最低付款金額是一個月十美金。
男：那聽起來太棒了！
vocabulary: Ex. She jogs every morning to get rid of her excess weight.
為了減肥，她每天早晨都去慢跑。
5. fall apart 變為碎塊；解體
Ex. The deserted building is slowly falling apart over time.
那棟廢棄大樓隨著時間久遠慢慢地碎裂。
6. match v. 相配；比賽/ n.比賽；對手
Ex. In the event of rain, the match will be postponed.
8. program n.方案；節目；程式/ v. 為...制定計劃

questions.stem: What's wrong with the bookcases and shelves?
translation: 那些書櫥跟架子怎麼了？
a. 有一些壞了。
b. 顏色太淺了。
c. 與公司的標誌不搭配。
d. 不夠大。
options: (C) They don't match with the company logo.

questions.stem: What kind of problem does the man mention they have?
translation: 該男子提到他們有什麼問題？
a. 財務問題。
b. 交往問題。
c. 找傢具店的問題。
d. 折扣問題。
options: (C) A problem finding the furniture store.

questions.stem: How long would it take to pay for a 100-dollar bookshelf if you pay the minimum each month?
translation: 如果每月付最低金額那要花多久來付清一個一百美金的書架？
a. 一個月。
b. 十個月。
c. 一年。
d. 沒有提到。

cards.passage:
M: If I send you my order by fax, is there a specific format you'd like me to use?
W: Actually, yes. We are pretty particular about the way we receive orders. The first page should clearly indicate the date and time of your order, as well as all your company details, including name, address, phone number, and contact person.
M: Got it. And what about the order itself?
W: You should use the order form available on our website. There are also directions online for how to fill it our properly.
M: Anything else I should know?
W: Yes. Please ensure that the pages are all numbered on the bottom left hand side. For example, if you are faxing a total of 7 sheets, the first one should indicate 1 over 7.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：如果我用傳真把我的訂單發給妳，妳有任何希望我使用的特定格式嗎？
女：事實上有。我們對於接受訂單的方式十分挑剔。第一頁必須清楚表明你的訂單的日期和時間，以及你公司的詳細資料，包括名字、地址、電話，和聯絡人。
男：了解。那訂單本身呢？
女：你應該用我們網站上放的訂購表單。我們網站上也有如何正確填寫它的指引。
男：還有什麼我該知道的嗎？
女：有。請確認所有的頁面左下方有標上頁碼。例如，如果你總共要傳七頁，第一頁應該標明七之一。
vocabulary: 1. specific adj.明確的；特有的/ n.特性
2. particular adj. 特殊的；詳細的/ n. 詳細情況；特點
Ex. It is a greater blessing to give than to receive.
7. properly adv. 正確地；體面地
Ex. This medicine will ensure you a good night's sleep.
這藥將保證你一晚好眠。

questions.stem: How does the woman describe her company's policy for receiving orders?
translation: 該女子如何形容她的公司接受訂單的原則？
a. 他們很挑剔。
b. 很隨意。
c. 頗方便。
d. 對使用者友善的。

questions.stem: How can the man get the order forms?
translation: 該男子要如何取得訂購表單？
a. 他必須自己仔細的填寫。
b. 他可以在網路上找得到。
c. 會傳真給他。
d. 該女子會提供。
options: (A) He must write them carefully by himself.
(B) He can find them on the net.
(C) They will be faxed to him.

questions.stem: Which requirement is not mentioned for the order forms?
translation: 那項對訂購表單的要求未被提到？
a. 頁碼。
b. 公司資料。
c. 訂購日期。
d. 紙張大小。

cards.passage:
M: Excuse me. Can you point me in the right direction for the restrooms?
W: There actually aren't any located on this floor. You'll have to grab the elevator down to the public facilities in the lobby. Alternatively, there's a flight of stairs down the hall next to room 202. Hop up one floor and you can ask the custodian staff to use theirs.
M: I have a recent knee injury, so I'll head down to the lobby. By the way, will I need my visitor's pass or some personal ID to get back in here?
W: I'll be at this desk the entire morning, so I'll remember you!
cards.passageTranslation:
男：打擾一下。可以請您指點我去廁所的正確方向嗎？
女：事實上這一樓沒有任何廁所。你必須搭電梯下樓到大廳的公共設施。或者，走廊下去202號房旁有一段樓梯。往上爬一層樓然後你可以要守衛讓你使用他們的。
男：我最近膝蓋受傷，所以我會下去大廳。對了，我會需要我的訪客證或是身份證之類的東西才能回到這裡嗎？
女：我整個上午都會在這個櫃台，所以我會記得你！
vocabulary: Ex. His house is located on the tip of the peninsula.
他的房子位於半島的頂端。
Ex. The entire operation is done by computer.
整個運作是由電腦完成的。

questions.stem: Where will the man go to use the washroom?
translation: 這名男子會去何處上廁所。
a. 大廳的廁所。
b. 樓上守衛的廁所。
c. 走廊下去的浴室。
d. 他目前所在的辦公室。
options: (C) The bathroom just down the hall.
(D) In the office where he is now.

questions.stem: What will he need to get back in?
translation: 他回來時會需要什麼？
a. 他的身份證。
b. 他的訪客證。
c. 他的簽名。
d. 都不用。

questions.stem: Why doesn't he want to use the stairs?
translation: 為何他不想走樓梯？
a. 太遠了。
b. 他不知該如何找到它們。
c. 他的腿受傷了。
d. 電梯比較快。
options: (B) He doesn't know how to find them.

cards.passage:
W: Hello. I'm calling on behalf of the Society of Compassion for Animals. May I please have a moment of your time?
M: I suppose. You're not selling anything are you?
W: No, I'm not. We are actually looking for donations or volunteers for our upcoming fundraiser event to get animals off the streets.
M: Well, I really support your cause. However, I'm quite short on cash these days so I don't think I'll be able to make a donation.
W: So how about volunteering? It will only take a few hours of your time, this Saturday, from 1-4pm at the Norwood Flower Market. We need people to watch the animals and hand our fliers.
M: I guess I could give that a shot.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：您好。我代表關懷動物協會致電。我能擔誤您一點時間嗎？
男：應該可以。你不是要賣什麼東西吧？
女：不，我不是。我們事實上是在為即將到來的拯救流浪動物募款活動找尋捐款或志工。
男：嗯，我完全支持你們的理念。但是，我最近很缺錢所以我不覺得我能夠捐款。
女：那擔任志工呢？它只要你花幾個小時的時間，這禮拜六，從下午一點到四點在諾伍德花市。我們需要人照顧動物和發傳單。
男：我想我可以試試看。
vocabulary: 5. volunteer n. 志願者；義工/ v. 自願
7. fundraiser n. 資金籌集活動(人)
8. support v./n. 支撐；支持；扶養
Ex. If we have any problems, they will support us.
到緊要關頭他們會支持我們的。
9. flier n. 廣告傳單；飛行員(物)
Ex. I know who you are and whom you represent.
我知道你是誰並代表誰。

questions.stem: What kind of group does the woman represent?

d. 他會志願參加他們的募款活動。

cards.passage:
M: Could you please describe to us your experience with selling smartphones and other mobile devices?
W: Actually, I haven't technically sold any smartphones or mobile devices in the past.
M: So why do you feel that you would be well suited for this position?
W: Well, I worked at Great Buy, which is a large department store that sells a variety of different electronics, including smartphones, tablets, cell phones, and the like. My specific department handled laptops specifically, so that was my area of expertise. However, I believe that the skills required for selling laptops and smartphones are similar, so I could quickly adapt to this position.
M: And are you very familiar with the brands and features of mobile devices?
W: Indeed, I am.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：能請你為我們描述你銷售智慧手機和其他行動裝置的經驗嗎？
女：其實，嚴格來說過去我還沒賣過任何智慧手機或行動裝置。
男：那你為什麼覺得自己會十分適合這個職位？
女：嗯，我在超特買工作過，那是一家大型百貨商場販賣各種類型的不同電子產品，包括智慧手機、平板電腦、行動電話，以及類似的產品。我的部門專門負責筆記型電腦，因此那是我的專精領域。不過，我認為銷售筆電與智慧手機所需的技能是類似的，所以我很快就可以上手。
男：那你對行動裝置的品牌和特色十分熟嗎？
女：沒錯，我是。
vocabulary: 1. experience n. 經驗/ v. 經驗；感受
3. suit v. 適合；相稱/ n. 衣服；訴訟
6. handle v. 處理；操作/ n. 把手
Ex. I'm old enough to handle my own affairs.
我已經長大可以處理我自己的事情。
7. expertise n. 專門知識(技術)
Ex. The novel was adapted for the screen.
這本小說被改編成電影。
9. position n. 位置；地位；姿態/ v. 把...放在適當位置
10. session n. 會議；講習；開庭

cards.passage:
M: What did you guys discuss in this morning's product development meeting?
W: Well, given the explosive popularity of Pokemon Go, we decided we need to come up with something similar, and the sooner the better.
M: That's smart. But we aren't just going to copy Pokemon Go, are we?
W: Not exactly. But we are still brainstorming ideas for making it different.
M: What would the characters be like?
W: We are trying to decide between using one of our classic characters such as Bob the Penguin, Ronald Robot, Alien Munchkins, and so on, or inventing new characters such as flying pencils, angry shapes, or bubble animals.
M: Cute. That's a tough call!
cards.passageTranslation:
男：你們在今天上午的產品開發會議上討論了什麼？
女：嗯，鑑於寶可夢爆紅的超人氣，我們決定我們需要推出類似的東西，而且越快越好。
男：很聰明。但是我們不是要複製寶可夢吧？
女：不完全是。但是，我們仍絞盡腦汁在想使它不同的想法。
男：那角色會是甚麼呢？
女：我們正嘗試決定要使用如鮑勃企鵝、羅納德機器人、孟奇金外星人等等我們的經典人物，或是如飛行鉛筆、憤怒形狀及氣泡動物這些新創造的角色。
男：真可愛。好難的選擇！
鮑勃企鵝
羅納德機器人
孟奇金外星人 飛行鉛筆
憤怒形狀
氣泡動物

b. 因為寶可夢真的很可愛。
c. 因為這個遊戲大受歡迎。
d. 寶可夢是他們主要競爭對手的產品。
options: (A) The product development team members like playing it.
(B) Because Pokemon Go is really cute.
(C) Due to the game's immense popularity.
(D) Pokemon Go is their main competitor's product.

questions.stem: How will this company's game be different from Pokemon Go?

a. 將有不同的設計。
b. 會使用不同的策略。
c. 它不會使用全球定位系統和地圖。
d. 還尚未確定。
options: (A) It will have a different design.
(C) It won't use GPS and maps.
(D) This has yet to be determined.

c.可能的新遊戲角色。

cards.passage:
M: Fashion Brothers. Marco speaking.
W: Hi, Marco. Anette from Thrifty's. How are you?
M: Great. And you? I saw you guys placed another order few days ago.
W: I'm fine as well. Yes, that's why I'm calling. I'd like to change one item on the order, but I'm not sure if the online tool is ready for doing that yet.
M: It actually is ready, but since I have you on the phone, I can help you with that.
W: Sure, but I will need written confirmation.
M: No problem. I'll send that to you later by email.
W: Great. So, for article number six on the order list, I'd like those women's T-shirts in light blue, not beige.
M: Right, so that's article number six, light blue instead of beige.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：時尚兄弟。我是Marco。
女：嗨，Marco。我是Thrifty的Anette。你好嗎？
男：好極了。你呢？我有看到你們前幾天又訂貨。
女：我也很好。是的，這就是為什麼我打電話來。我想變更訂單中一件商品，但我不確定線上工具可不以用來改單。
男：實際上是可以用了，但既然我接到你的電話，我可以幫你處理。
女：好的，但我需要書面確認。
男：沒問題。我待會通過電子郵件發送給你。
女：太好了。那訂單中的貨號6號，我要淺藍色女T，不是米色。
男：好，所以就是貨號6號，淺藍色而不是米色。
訂單
客戶名稱：Thrifty’s
接收日期：5月1日
商品清單：
1. 橘色男士polo衫（數量12）
2. 海軍藍男士polo衫（數量12）
3. 白色男士T卹（數量20）
4. 黑色女士運動褲（數量為10）
5. 綠色女士卡其褲（數量10）
6. 米色女士T卹（數量20）

questions.stem: What do we know about Thrifty's?

c. 他們之前有從時尚兄弟訂購過。
d. 他們跟時尚兄弟位於同一棟大樓。
options: (A) They are a subsidiary of Fashion Brothers.

cards.passage:
M: Can you please tell us why you'd like to cancel your card?
W: I just find that I don't use it enough to justify the annual fee.
M: Have you considered our simplified account option? The fee is only $10 a year, and it comes with most of the benefits of the regular account.
W: So what exactly is different about it?
M: The only major difference is that it doesn't include the option of online banking.
W: I think I'm still going to pass.
cards.passageTranslation:
M：能否請你告訴我們為什麼想取消你的信用卡嗎？
W：我只是覺得我用這張信用卡不夠需支付的年費。
M：你是否考慮過我們的"簡化帳戶"選項？費用每年只有10美元，帳戶備有常用帳戶的益處。
W：那到底有什麼不同之處呢？
M：唯一的主要區別是，這個帳戶不可使用網路銀行。
W：我想我還是不要。
vocabulary: 1. exchange v. 交換; n. 交換；交易；匯率
2. dimension n. 尺寸；面積；範圍；特點
4. definitely adv. 明確地；肯定地
5. chart n. 圖表/ v. 繪製圖表；詳細計劃
6. regulation n. 規章; 調整; a. 標準的; 普通的
Ex. The new regulations should be brought into effect as soon as possible.
這些新規則應該儘快付之實行。
7. headquarters n. 總部 * headquarter v. 設立總部
8. gear n. 設備；齒輪/v. 搭上齒輪適應
Ex. We stowed our camping gear in the garage.
我們把野營用具儲藏在車庫裡。
Ex. His explanation clarified the mystery.
他的說明解開了這個謎。
10. justify v. 辯護；證明...是正當的
Ex. How can you justify your rude behavior?
你如何解釋你的粗魯行為呢?
12. It comes with… 它帶有(包含)
13. regular adj. 規則的；定期的；經常的；一般的

cards.passage:
W: Hello. What is the cost for a manicure and pedicure?
M: They are 19.99 each, but if you plan to get both, I would strongly recommend you go for the Total Makeover Package for 44.99, plus tax.
W: What else does that include?
M: Besides the manicure and pedicure, you get a full facial. Then we finish you off with a 20-minute shoulder and neck massage. The whole process generally takes about 2 hours.
W: Is it possible to upgrade to a full-body massage?
M: Of course. We can make that an hour-long, full body massage for an additional 20 dollars, bringing the total to 64.99, plus tax.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：你好。請問修手跟腳趾甲要多少錢？
男：他們分別是19.99元，不過你若是兩個都要的話，我會強烈建議您選44.99元含稅的徹底美容組合。
女：那裏頭還包括什麼？
男：除了修手指跟腳趾甲外，還有全套臉部美容。最後結束時有個20分鐘的肩頸按摩。整個過程一般要花大約2小時。
女：能升級成全身按摩嗎？
男：當然可以。加價20元我們可以改成一小時長的全身按摩，總共加起來是64.99元含稅。
vocabulary: 1. reservation n. 預訂；保留；保護區
Ex. We have made reservations for three rooms at the hotel.
我們已在這個旅館預訂了三個房間。
2. Jacuzzi n. 浴池(周邊可噴水按摩的小浴池, 源自商標名)
Ex. Trade connections existed between the two countries before the war.
戰前兩國之間有過貿易往來。
4. laundry n. 洗衣店；洗好的衣服
Ex. The post office is just across from the club.
郵局就在那俱樂部對面。
6. Thanks a million. 萬分感謝
Ex. The ethics of his profession do not permit him to do that.
他的職業道德不容許他那樣做。
8. manicure n. 修指甲/v. 替...修指甲
9. pedicure n. 修腳趾甲；足部治療
10. facial adj. 臉的; 表面的/ n. 臉部按摩(美容)
11. upgrade v. 升級; 提高/ n. v.升級；提高

cards.passage:
M: Are you almost finished with this meeting room?
W: I'm sorry, but I believe that we have booked it for the entire afternoon.
M: Actually, according to this booking form, we have reserved it from 1 to 3 o'clock for our product review seminar.
W: Let me have a look at that. Oh, no wonder. You are looking at the wrong day! Today is Tuesday, not Monday!
M: Oh gosh! I forgot that yesterday was a national holiday!
W: These things happen. I would suggest that you get in touch with Sydney upstairs. She usually knows which rooms are available in the building.
M: Will do. Sorry to interrupt your interview.
cards.passageTranslation:
M：會議室快好了嗎？
W：我很抱歉，但我以為我們已經預訂了整個下午。
M：其實，根據預訂表格，我們已預留1至3點鐘要召開產品審查研討會。
W：讓我看看表格。哦，怪不得。您看錯日期了！今天是星期二，不是星期一！
M：哦，天哪！我忘了昨天是國定假日！
W：這種事常發生。我會建議你和樓上的Sydney多聯絡。她通常都會知道大樓裡哪一間有空檔。
M：我會的。很抱歉打斷你的面談。

questions.stem: What was the man's mistake?

a. 他訂的房間日期出錯。
b. 他以為仍是週末。
c. 他訂錯房間。
d. 他事實上沒有訂房。
options: (A) He booked the room for the wrong date.
(B) He thought it was still the weekend.

questions.stem: What kind of meeting does the man need to have?
translation: 男子需要什麼樣的會議？
a. 產品評論研討會。
b. 接受採訪。
c. 深入的討論會。
d. 沒有說明。

questions.stem: What might Sydney be able to do?

a. 重新安排採訪。
b. 將女子踢出房間。
c. 將她的辦公室借給男子。
d. 幫他們找個新的房間以供使用。
options: (B) Kick the woman out of the room.
(D) Find them a new room to use.

cards.passage:
W: Excuse me. I'm new to this country. Is it customary to tip the waiters and waitresses here?
M: Yes, we go by the old 10% rule, but in fact, people tend to tip upwards of 15-20% these days, provided the service was actually decent.
W: Is the service charge ever included on the bill?
M: Yes, that is something to look out for. If it is, you are not expected to tip on top of that, unless you truly want to.
W: Thank you, that information is invaluable to me.
M: No worries! By the way, how long are you here for?
W: I'm just on an overnight stopover en route to Paris.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：抱歉。我不是這個郡的人。請問這裏的習慣是會給男女服務生小費的嗎？
男：是的，照一成的老規矩，不過事實上，如果服務好的話現在大家多半會往上給到15至20%
女：服務費可能會包括在帳單上嗎？
男：是的，那是要特別注意的部分。如果有的話，你不必須要再加上小費，除非你真的想這麼作。
女：謝謝你，這些資訊對我無比貴重。
男：別客氣！對了，你要在這裡多久？
女：我只是在往巴黎途中作過夜停留。

questions.stem: What is the '10% rule'?
translation: 什麼是'一成的規矩'？
a. 餐廳加在帳單上的一成服務費。
b. 10%的附加稅。
c. 至少必須給10%的規矩。
d. 給大約一成小費的一般習慣。
options: (A) A service charge of 10% that the restaurant puts on the bill.
(C) A rule that you must leave at least 10%.
(D) A common custom of tipping about 10%.

questions.stem: What happens if the service charge is on the bill?
translation: 如果帳單上有服務費了該如何？
a. 你應該給額外的10%。
b. 你應該給額外的15到20%。
c. 你必須要付更多。
d. 你不需要多付錢，除非你願意。
options: (C) You are expected to pay more.
(D) You don't need to pay more, unless you want.

questions.stem: How long is the woman staying in this country?
translation: 這位女士要在這個郡停留多久？
a. 她沒說。
b. 一週或更久。
c. 幾個小時。
d. 一晚。

cards.passage:
W: After carefully reviewing your application, I'm afraid we have to decline your application for research funding.
M: May I ask what the reason is for the rejection?
W: Unfortunately, the department is low on funds this semester. Your project is pretty costly, and some of the goals were pretty unclear.
M: Will I have an opportunity to reapply next semester?
W: Yes, we would strongly encourage you to reapply after the summer vacation when we have a new budget. That will also give you some time to polish up your application papers.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：在仔細審查你的申請書後，我恐怕我們必須要拒絕您的研究經費申請。
男：我能問拒絕的原因嗎？
女：很不幸的，單位裏這一學年經費不足。你的計畫滿花錢的，而且某些目標不甚明確。
男：我會有機會在下個學年重新申請嗎？
女：有，我會強烈鼓勵你在暑假過後當我們有新的經費時重新申請。那也可給你一些時間修飾你的申請文件。
vocabulary: 1. tense adj. 緊張的；繃緊的/v. 拉緊；繃緊
Ex. The situation suddenly became tense.
局勢突然變得緊張起來。
2. time and time again一次又一次
Ex. In May 1968, there was a massive wave of student riots.
一九六八年五月曾發生過大規模的學生騷亂。
5. pleasant adj. 令人愉快；舒適的
6. 低廉的 v. 離開；背離；去世
7. boarding v. 上船(或火車、飛機等)；木板
Ex. Our flight is boarding at gate number 4.
我們的航班在四號門登機。
8. decline v. 下降；衰退；謝絕/n. 下降; 衰退
Ex. Unemployment declined to 4 percent last month.
上個月失業率降至百分之四。
10. rejection n. 拒絕；廢棄(物) ；排斥
11. costly adj. 貴重的；昂貴的
12. encourage v. 鼓勵；促進；支持
14. budget n. 預算/v. 編列預算/adj.低廉的

questions.stem: What was the man hoping to receive today?
translation: 該男子今天期待得到的是甚麼？
a. 一些研究的經費。
b. 他的計畫的資訊。
c. 研究的新目標。
d. 長一點的暑假。
options: (C) A new goal to work on.

questions.stem: What was one problem with his application?
translation: 他的申請書有什麼問題？
a. 太簡短。
b. 他什麼都沒填。
c. 他沒有把目標說明得很明確。
d. 太晚繳交了。
options: (C) He didn't state his goals clearly.
(D) It was handed in too late.

questions.stem: Why might he have better luck next semester?
translation: 為什麼他下個學年運氣會較好？
a. 他可能遇到不同的審查員。
b. 該部門會有更多錢。
c. 他可以多加修飾他的研究數據。
d. 他會有更大的預算來支付更好的申請案。
options: (A) He might get a different reviewer.
(B) The department will have more money.
(C) He can use more polish on his research data.
(D) He will have a larger budget to pay for a better application.

cards.passage:
M: I'd like to exchange this travel luggage for a different one.
W: Is there something wrong with it or you just didn't like it?
M: Actually, the salesperson that helped me told me that it was small enough to be considered carry on. However, when I got home and checked the dimensions, I found that it is actually too large to be used for carry on with most airlines.
W: I sincerely apologize for that mistake, sir. I know for a fact that you are indeed correct. This bag is definitely too large for carry on. I would be happy to show you which ones you are looking for. I even have a chart here that lists various international airlines and their carry on regulations.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：我想要把這個行李箱換成另一個。
女：它有什麼不好的地方或者你只是不喜歡呢？
男：事實上那個幫我的銷售員告訴我它小到足以被視為是隨身行李。然而當我回到家量過尺寸後，我發現它其實太大而在多數的航空公司無法當作隨身行李使用。
女：我誠摯的為這個疏失道歉向您道歉。我知道事實上您的確是對的。這個包包作為登機箱確實太大了。我很樂意為您展示你所想要的。我這裡甚至有一張表列出多家國際航空公司的隨身行李規定。

questions.stem: In what kind of place is this conversation happening?
translation: 這段談話發生在什麼地方？
a. 機場。
b. 航空公司總部。
c. 旅遊用品店。
d. 雜貨店。

questions.stem: What is wrong with the baggage?
translation: 行李箱出了甚麼錯誤了嗎？
a. 行李箱太小無法辦理登機手續。
b. 過大無法攜帶。
c. 行李箱壞掉了。
d. 行李箱是由劣質材料做的。
options: (A) It is too small to check in.
(B) It is too large to carry on.
(D) It is made of poor quality materials.

questions.stem: What additional information will the clerk provide?
translation: 店員提供什麼樣的額外訊息？
a.最優質的航空公司圖表。
b. 航空公司的規定列表。
c. 行李選項的冊本。
d. 退貨政策的說明。
options: (A) A chart of the best airlines.
(D) An explanation of the return policy.

cards.passage:
W: You guys seem to be experts with cameras. Is there a certain brand or model that you would recommend?
M: Well, that depends if you're looking for something basic, or more professional?
W: I was thinking of investing in a DSLR, but nothing too fancy or complicated.
M2: Well the best entry-level DSLR is the Canon 600D, in my opinion.
W: Why do you say that one specifically?
M: Well, it is user friendly, relatively affordable, and just as good as other more expensive models for somebody who doesn't plan to shoot too seriously.
M2: Personally, I'm a Nikon guy. The Nikon D5100 is the equivalent to the one Marc is talking about.
W: So which one is better?
M: To each his own.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：你們似乎對照相機很專業。你有什麼推薦的品牌或機型嗎？
男：嗯，那要看你想要的是基本款或更專業一點的？
女：我在考慮要買台數位單眼相機，但是不要太炫或複雜的。
男2：以我來看，最佳的入門款數位單眼相機是Canon 600D。
女：你為什麼特別指定那台呢？
男：嗯，它使用親合度高、比較實惠，而且對不打算要拍很嚴肅題材的人來說它與其它更昂貴的機種一樣棒。
男2：就我本身來說，我是Nikon的粉絲。Nikon D5100和Marc正在在講的那台一樣好。
女：那麼…哪一台比較好？
男：這兩台都是它們品牌最好的。
vocabulary: 1. customary adj. 習慣上的；合乎習俗的
Ex. Teachers tend to be partial towards students with good scores.
老師往往容易偏袒成績優良的學生。
3. upward adj. 向上的; adv. 向上地；以上地
4. decent adj. 正派的；體面的
Ex. He is a decent, upstanding young man.
他是一個正派誠實的年輕人。
8. professional adj. 職業的；擅長的/ n. 職業選手；專家
10. fancy n. 迷戀；想像; v. 猜想；喜愛; adj. 別緻的；昂貴的；想像出來的
Ex. He fancies himself as a poet. 他自以為是詩人。
11. entry-level adj. 入門的；基本的
12. in my opinion 依我之見
Ex. In my opinion, this price is reasonable.
依我之見，這個價格很合理。
13. specifically adv. 特別地；明確地
15. feature n. 特徵；面貌/v. 以...為特色
16. equally adv. 相同地；公平地
Ex. We must work hard, but equally we must get sufficient rest.
我們必須努力工作,但同時也要有足夠的休息。

questions.stem: What kind of camera is the woman looking to purchase?
translation: 這位女士想要買哪一種的相機？
a. 很炫的。
b. 有許多複雜功能的。
c. 基本型的數位單眼相機。
d. 現有最專業的品牌的。
options: (B) One that has many complicated features.

questions.stem: Based on the second man's comments, what's the difference between the Canon 600D and the Nikon D5100?
translation: 根據第二名男子的意見，Canon 600D和Nikon D5100之間的區別為何？
a. Canon的價格較為親切。
b. Nikon使用比較容易。
c. Canon整體品質較佳。
d. 兩者只是品牌不同。
options: (B) The Nikon is easier to use.
(C) The Canon has been overall quality.

questions.stem: What does Marc means at the end?
translation: Marc最後的寓意為何？
a. Canon是她最好的選擇。
b. 他無法再幫助她了。
c. 不同的人喜歡不同的物品。
d. 他覺得他自己的相機是最好的。
options: (A) The Canon is her best choice.
(B) He can't really help her anymore.
(D) He thinks his own camera is the best one.

cards.passage:
W: You look very tense. What's wrong?
M: I'm embarrassed to admit this, but I am scared of flying.
W: Really? Is it your first time?
M: I have flown several times, but time and time again I get a wave of nervousness just before take off. I'm not even worried about crashing or anything. It's just a terrible feeling in my insides.
W: I had an aunt with the same problem. She told me the best thing to prevent the feeling was to close her eyes, breathe deeply, and think of a pleasant memory from her childhood.
M: Maybe I'll give it a shot and see how it works for me.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：你看起來很緊張。有什麼不對勁嗎？
男：我不太好意思承認，我害怕搭飛機。
女：真的？這是你第一次嗎？
男：我搭過幾次了，可是我總是一在的在起飛前覺得緊張。我甚至不會去擔心失事或什麼的。就只是內心的感受很糟。
女：我有個姑姑有相同的問題。她告訴我要避免這個感覺得最佳方法是閉上眼睛、深呼吸，然後回想兒時愉快的記憶。
男：或許我會試一下這個辦法看看對我有沒有效。

questions.stem: Why is the man so nervous?
translation: 該男子為何如此緊張？
a. 這是他第一次上飛機。
b. 他恐懼飛行。
c. 他下飛機後有個壓力沉重的會議。
d. 他上次搭飛機有不好的記憶。
options: (A) It's his first time on an airplane.
(B) He has a fear of flying.
(C) He has a stressful meeting after the flight.
(D) He has a bad memory from a previous flight.

questions.stem: Which does the woman not mention to help the man?
translation: 哪個可幫助該男子的是這位女士沒有提到的？
a. 試著不要去想有關於失事。
b. 閉上他的眼睛。
c. 深呼吸。
d. 試著回想好的回憶。
options: (A) Try not to think about crashing.
(D) Try to remember a good memory.

questions.stem: When does the man get most worried?
translation: 該男子最緊張的是甚麼時候？
a. 登機的時。
b. 飛行途中。
c. 剛要出發時。
d. 飛機剛要起飛時。
options: (D) Just before the plane takes off.

cards.passage:
W: Check please!
M: Rhonda, let me get this one.
W: No, Dennis. It's my treat.
M: You got it last time though! Plus, I have a VIP card for this restaurant. I can get us a 10% discount.
W: Well, if you insist. But I'm getting the coffees on the way back to work.
M: It's a deal. Don't forget we said we'd bring some back for Linda and Craig.
W: Oh, yes! Do you remember what they asked for?
M: Linda wanted a plain black coffee. Craig wanted a latte with one sugar.
W: You have a good memory.
M: Not really. They ask for the same thing every time I offer.
cards.passageTranslation:
女 : 買單！
男：Rhonda，我來付。
女：不，Dennis。我請客。
男：上次是你付的！而且，我有這家餐廳的貴賓卡，可以打九折。
女：好吧，如果你堅持。但回去上班的路上買咖啡由我來付。
男：成交。別忘了我們說會幫Linda和Craig帶咖啡回去。
女 : 哦，對！你還記得他們要喝什麼嗎？
男：Linda要一杯黑咖啡。Craig要一杯拿鐵加一匙糖。
女 : 你記性真好。
男：並沒有。他們每次要的都一樣。
黑傑克牛排餐廳
客戶貴賓卡
在付款時出示此卡可享有任何餐點10％的折扣。
*最多兩位
*優惠不包含酒精飲料、稅金以及服務費

questions.stem: What are the two speakers arguing about at the beginning?
translation: 以下選項何者是兩位發言者一開始在爭論的重點？
a. 誰應該去拿咖啡。
b. 他們應該使用哪張卡付費。
c. 應該在何處吃午飯。
d. 應該由誰買單。
options: (A) Who should pick up the coffee.
(B) Which card they should use to pay.
(D) Who should pay for the meal.

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. Whose card is this?
translation: 請見圖表。這是誰的卡片 ？
a. Rhonda的
b. Dennis的
c. Linda的
d. Craig的

questions.stem: Why does the man remember what kind of coffee Linda and Craig asked for?
translation: 以下選項何者是該男子記得Linda和Craig要喝什麼咖啡的原因？
a. 他有寫下來。
b. 他和他們都喜歡同一種咖啡。
c. 他之前有幫他們買過這些咖啡。
d. 他的記性很好。
options: (B) He likes the same kind of coffee that they do.
(C) He has picked up these coffees for them before.

cards.passage:
M: Hi, I have a reservation under Mr. Walters.
W: Let's see... Mr. Walters... Here it is! A single room, non-smoking, with flat screen TV and Jacuzzi.
M: That's the one! Is there an Internet connection in the bedroom?
W: I'm afraid our Wi-Fi is down at the moment. It should be up and running in a few hours. For now, you can get online in our 24-hour business center on the second floor, across from the laundry rooms.
M: Sounds good. Oh! I nearly forgot to ask. What time is your restaurant open until?
W: They serve food until 10pm, and beverages until midnight.
M: Great, thanks a million.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：你好，我有用華特先生的名字訂房。
女：我看一下。華特先生…在這裡！一間單人房、非吸菸、有平面螢幕電視跟按摩浴缸。
男：沒錯！房裡可以連上網路嗎？
女：很抱歉我們的無線網路目前停用。它在幾個小時內應該可以恢復正常。現在要用的話您可以在我們二樓24小時開放的商務中心裏上網，就在洗衣房的對面。
男：聽起來不錯。喔！我差點忘了問。你們的餐廳營業到甚麼時候？
女：他們食物供應到晚上10點，飲料供應到午夜。
男：棒極了，非常感謝。

questions.stem: Which of the following is not permitted in the man's hotel room?
translation: 這位男士的旅館房間裏不允許下列何者？
a. 上網。
b. 作生意。
c. 泡按摩浴缸。
d. 抽菸。

questions.stem: Where is the business center located in the hotel?
translation: 商務中心位在旅館的何處？
a. 大廳裏。
b. 洗衣房對面。
c. 在三樓。
d. 他們目前沒有。
options: (D) They don't have one at the moment.

questions.stem: How late can you get a drink in the hotel restaurant?
translation: 到多晚還可以在旅館的餐廳裏喝到飲料？
a. 晚上10點。
b. 中午12點。
c. 凌晨12點。
d. 24小時都可以。

cards.passage:
W: North Star Cinema. How can I help you?
M: Hi, I'm trying to reserve a seat online for the 6:40 PM showing of Lost in Paradise, but the system isn't letting me choose a seat.
W: Are you by any chance trying to use a voucher?
M: Yes, actually.
W: Some of our vouchers don't allow a seat reservation. Basically how it works is you can redeem it for a free ticket to any movie, provided there are available seats in the theater.
M: So I just show up at the theater and hope that there is a seat available for what I want to see?
W: Essentially, yes. You can start buying tickets an hour before any movie showing. And Lost in Paradise has been playing for some time now, so it usually doesn't sell out.
M: Good to know. Thank you.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：北極星影城。 有什麼能為您服務？
男：嗨，我試著在網上預訂下午6:40放映的迷失在天堂的座位，但系統不讓我選擇座位。
女：你可能使用優惠券了？
男：是的，沒錯。
女：我們有一些券不能預訂座位。基本上使用方式是，只要有空位，你就可以兌換任何一部電影的免費電影票。
男：所以我就只要出現電影院，希望我想看的電影有空位嗎？
女：大體上是這樣。您可以在任何一部電影放映前一個小時買票。而迷失在天堂已經播放了一段時間了，所以它的電影票通常賣不完。
男：很高興知道。 謝謝。
電影放映時間
電影的名字：迷失在天堂
每天放映場數：4
時間：4:40，6:40，7:30，9:15

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. Which showing of Lost in Paradise does the man hope to see?
translation: 請見圖表。男子想看的電影-迷失在天堂是以下哪個放映場次?
a. 第一個
b. 第二個
c. 第三個
d. 第四個

questions.stem: Why couldn't the man book a seat online?
translation: 為什麼男子不能在網上訂位？
a. 該系統已關閉。
b. 優惠券不能用於預訂座位。
c. 他試圖在電影放映一小時以上前訂票。
d. 他的方法錯誤。
options: (B) Vouchers can't be used to book a seat.
(C) He was trying to do it more than one hour before the movie showing time.

questions.stem: What does the woman imply at the end?
translation: 以下選項何者是該女子在結尾時所表達的意思？
a. 他來的時候應該會有座位。
b. 這部電影的座位總是銷售一空。
c. 他應該至少在電影開場前一個小時到。
d. 這部電影不值得看。
options: (A) He should be able to get a seat when he comes.
(B) Seats for this movie always sell out.
(C) He should come at least an hour before it starts.

cards.passage:
W: Sir, excuse me. Do you have a moment? I have a few questions about these printers.
M: Certainly. How can I help you?
W: What's the main difference between these two? The black one is double the price, but seems to have all the same functions.
M: The main difference is mainly the brand name. The black one is a reputable brand, but in all honestly, the white one is just as good.
W: Got it. Does the white one print in color or only black and white?
M: Both. It also has scanning, copying, and faxing options. It's probably our top-selling printer.
W: Great. Well, I'm just going to shop around a little more, and I'll probably be back for it.
M: Do you want me to leave it at the counter for you? I think this is our last one in stock at the moment.
cards.passageTranslation:
W：先生，不好意思。你有時間嗎？我對這些印表機有些問題。
M：當然可以。我能幫你什麼？
W：這兩台機器間的主要區別是什麼？黑色的是雙倍的價錢，但似乎功能相同。
M：區別主要是品牌名稱。黑色是個有信譽的品牌，但老實說，白色的功能一樣好。
W：明白了。白色的印表機是彩色列印嗎？或只有黑白兩色？
M：都有。它還兼有掃描，複印，傳真選項。這可能是我們最暢銷的印表機。
W：好極了。好吧，我只是要再多看一看，我可能等下就會回來。
M：你要我把這台留在櫃台嗎？我想這是我們目前最後一台。
vocabulary: Ex. Today is her birthday according to the solar calendar.
按照陽曆，今天是她的生日。
3. get in touch with取得聯繫
Ex. I had no difficulty in getting in touch with him.
我和他保持聯繫並沒有什麼困難。
4. in-depth adj. 深入的；徹底的
6. function n. 功能；職務/v. 運行；行使職責
7. reputable adj. 聲譽好的；可尊敬的；標準的
Ex. His mother scanned his face to see if he was telling the truth.
他母親察看他的面色看他是不是在講真話。
11. debate n. 辯論(會)/v. 辯論；思考
Ex. They will debate face to face. 他們將當面進行辯論。
12. model n. 模型；模範；模特兒/v. 做模型/adj. 用作模型的；模範的
Ex. She models herself after her mother.
13. thoroughly adv. 徹底地；完全地

questions.stem: What's better about the black printer?
translation: 黑色的印表機為何比較好？
a. 它比較便宜。
b. 使用時很好上手。
c. 它有多彩印刷也有黑色和白色。
d. 什麼也沒有。只有品牌名稱比較有名。
options: (C) It prints in color as well as black and white.
(D) Nothing. Only the name is more famous.

questions.stem: Will the woman buy the white printer?
translation: 女子會買的白色的印表機嗎？
a. 是的，她會的。
b. 不，她不會。
c. 她還在思考買黑色還是白色的。
d. 她要再想一想。
options: (C) She's still debating between the black and the white one.
(D) She wants to think about it for a while.

questions.stem: What does the man offer to do?
translation: 男子提供什麼？
a. 給她折扣。
b. 確保沒有其他人會購買。

cards.passage:
W: Where are you headed today, sir?
M: Buenos Aires.
W: How many pieces of check-in luggage?
M: None.
W: I'm afraid you can't carry that tripod on the plane. You'll have to check it in.
M: Really? But it's not very big.
W: It's not due to size. They are on the list of restricted items because they could be used as a weapon.
M: I don't want it to get damaged though.
W: I'll put a fragile sticker on it. Don't worry. We check them through all the time.
cards.passageTranslation:
女： 先生您今天要飛哪裡？
男： 布宜諾艾利斯 。
女： 有幾件行李要托運？
男： 沒有。
女： 恐怕你不能把那個三腳架帶上飛機。你得托運。
男： 真的嗎？可是它不是很大。
女： 不是因為大小的關係。它列在禁止項目的清單上，因為可以被拿來當成武器。
男： 可是我不想要它被弄壞了。
女： 我會在上面貼一張易碎物品的貼紙。別擔心。我們經常托運易碎物品。

questions.stem: What is the woman's job?
translation: 女子的職業為何？
a. 移民官
b. 海關官員
c. 機場警衛
d. 航空公司報到櫃檯職員

questions.stem: Why is the item not allowed to be carried onto the plane?
translation: 為什麼這個物品不能被帶上飛機？
a. 太大件。
b. 塞不進男子的登機袋中。
c. 基於安全考量而不允許。
d. 不是太貴重。
options: (B) It doesn't fit in the man's carry-on bag.
(C) It's not allowed for security reasons.

questions.stem: What reassurance does the woman give the man at the end?
translation: 女子最後怎麼保證讓男子放心？
a. 這是很常見的情況。
b. 他們有無受損的保障。
c. 東西可以換新。
d. 東西會被存放在一個硬盒子裏。
options: (B) They have a guarantee it won't be damaged.
(D) The item will be stored in a hard case.

cards.passage:
M: I can't believe it. My time is up with this company.
W: What do you mean? Are you getting fired?
M: Yes.
W: But your PIP period isn't even finished yet. You still have a chance!
M: Nope. My manager hinted to me that there's no way I'm going to make it. My numbers are still way too low. I bet he has already drafted my notification of termination.
W: Wow. I'm sorry to hear that. What did you say to him?
M: Not much. I'm going to save all my thoughts for the exit interview. Then I'm really going to speak my mind and give him an earful!
cards.passageTranslation:
男：我真不敢相信。 我在這家公司的大限已到。
女：你是什麼意思？你要被解雇了嗎？
男：是的。
女：但你的績效提高計劃期限還沒到。 你還有機會！
男：不。 我的經理暗示我是不可能完成的。 我的數字仍然太低。 我敢說他已經在草擬解雇通知了。
女：哇。 我很遺憾聽到這個消息。 你跟他說了什麼？
男：不多。 我要等離職面談時再說。 然後我會真正告訴他我的想法且臭罵他一頓！
終止就職的步驟
步驟 期間/時間 1.第一次預警期 六個月 2.績效提高計劃 三個月 3. 績效提高計劃期結束的一周內 4.離職面談 最後在職日前一天

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. What is the missing step?
translation: 請見圖表。缺少的步驟是什麼?
a. 試用期
b. 第二次警告期
c. 正式解職
d. 解雇通知

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. Which step is the man currently in?
translation: 請見圖表。男子目前正處於以下哪一步驟？

questions.stem: How does the man know he will definitely be fired?
translation: 該男子怎麼知道他肯定會被解僱？
a. 他的主管已經直接告訴他了。
b. 他的主管間接地告訴他了。
c. 他的主管已經填寫了解雇文件。
d. 他的主管告訴一些人。
options: (A) His manager already told him directly.
(C) His manager already filled out the termination documents.
(D) His manager told some other people.

cards.passage:
M: Dale Watkins, HTM Building. Do you know the extension number of the person you are trying to reach?
W: Yes, it's 445.
M: Hmm…I'm sorry. It looks like that extension number doesn't exist. Do you know the person's name?
W: It's been a while since I called. Perhaps the number has changed. It's Mr. Chien. But I'm afraid I don't recall his first name.
M: Let me see. I have two Mr. Chien's listed as working in the building here. One is a Mr. Bradley Chien, a physiologist. Another is Mr. Rui-He Chien, an orthopedic surgeon.
W: Honestly, neither of those sounds right. He's an infant pediatrician.
M: Oh! You are talking about Mr. Honda Chien. I believe he retired last year.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：Dale Watkins，HTM大樓。 您知道您想聯繫哪個分機號碼嗎？
女：是的，是445。
男：嗯...抱歉。 看來這分機號碼不存在。 你知道那個人的名字嗎？
女：我很久沒打電話來了， 也許號碼改了。 是秦先生。 但恐怕我不記得他的名字。
男：讓我看看。 我這邊顯示有兩位秦先生在這棟大樓工作。 一個是生理學家Bradley。 另一個是整形外科醫生琴瑞河先生。
女：老實說，聽來這些都不是。 他是一名嬰幼兒科醫生。
男：哦！ 你在說Honda秦先生。 我想他去年退休了。
HTM大樓分機號碼列表
琴瑞河
#195
Arnold Peters
#671
Bradley Chien
#812

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. Which of these extensions will the man transfer the woman to?
translation: 請見圖表。該男會幫女士轉到哪個分機？
d. 以上皆非

questions.stem: What kind of building is this?

a. 學校

questions.stem: What do we know about Honda Chien?
translation: 以下關於Honda Chien的敘述何者正確？
a. 他是老人。

c. 他在4樓工作。
d. 他是一個生理學家。

cards.passage:
W: Hi. What's the cover charge tonight?
M: Five bucks. But there's free cover before midnight if you check in on Facebook.
W: How do I do that?
M: Just find our page and you'll see the option to check in.
W: Do I have to include a comment?
M: Only if you want to.
W: Ok, done. Here, look.
M: Looks fine. Here, just let me stamp your hand. Last call is at 2 AM, and doors close at 3. Bar is on the left, dance floor in the middle, and coat check on the right. Have a good night.
cards.passageTranslation:
女： 嗨。今晚服務費是多少？
男： 五塊錢。可是如果你在臉書打卡可以免費。
女： 要怎麼做？
男： 找到我們的網頁然後你就會看到打卡的選項。
女： 我得要留言嗎？
男： 隨意。
女： 好, 完成。拿去，你看一下。
男： 看起來沒問題。我幫你在手上蓋章。最後一輪點酒時間是凌晨兩點，三點打烊。吧台在左邊，舞池在中間，衣帽間在右邊。祝妳有個愉快的夜晚。

questions.stem: What kind of establishment is this?

a. 夜總會

questions.stem: Which of the following is true based on the dialogue?
translation: 根據這段交談內容下列何者正確？
a. 這裡一直都是免費入場。
b. 過晚上十二點後才免費入場。
c. 要幫他們打廣告才可以免費入場。
d. 只有女生才可免費入場。
options: (B) Entry is only free after midnight.
(C) Entry is only free provided you help them advertise.
(D) Entry is only free for girls.

cards.passage:
M: What do you want to see tonight?
W: That romantic comedy we got last time was awful. How about this documentary on whales? It got really good reviews.
M: Sure. Look, the poster says we can get another new release for 50% off.
W: Yeah, but don't forget they are due back tomorrow. Are we really going to watch two tonight? It's already after 9.
M: Good point. OK. Forget it. But I'm getting microwave popcorn.
cards.passageTranslation:
男： 你今晚想看什麼？
女： 我們上次看的那部浪漫喜劇片糟透了。這部鯨魚的紀錄片如何？它頗受好評。
男： 好。你看，海報上說我們看另一部新片可以打五折。
女： 沒錯，可是別忘了那期限是明天要還。你今晚真的打算看兩片嗎？現在已經過了九點。
男： 有道理。好，那就算了。但我要買微波爆米花。

questions.stem: Where are the speakers?
translation: 說話者在何處？
a. 電影院

questions.stem: Why won't they get two?

a. 因為價格的關係
b. 因為品質
c. 因為他們沒有時間
d. 因為他們缺乏耐心
options: (C) because of their lack of time
(D) because of their lack of patience

questions.stem: What sudden decision does the man make at the end?
translation: 男子在最後突然做了什麼決定？
a. 買零食
b. 挑一部不一樣的電影
c. 反對他女友的決定
d. 付現金

cards.passage:
W: Wait a minute! What are you doing?
M: I'm writing up a ticket for this car.
W: Please don't! I only had to stop for a minute to drop my mother off.
M: Sorry, ma'am, but this space is for emergency vehicles only. No exceptions.
W: Is there anything I could do to make you change your mind? I can't get another ticket or I'll lose my license.
M: No.
W: How about one hundred dollars, right now.
M: Bribing a police officer is a serious offense, ma'am. Would you like to be arrested right now?
W: How about two hundred?
cards.passageTranslation:
女： 等一下！你在做什麼？
男： 我正在給這部車開罰單。
女： 拜託不要！我只是要停一會讓我媽媽下車。
男： 抱歉，小姐，可是這個地方只可以停緊急用車輛。沒有例外。
女： 我可以做什麼事來讓你改變主意嗎？我不能再吃罰單了，不然我會被吊銷執照。
男： 沒有。
女： 一百美金怎麼樣，現在給你。
男： 行賄警察是重罪，小姐。你想被立刻逮捕嗎？
女： 兩百呢？

questions.stem: What violation did the woman make?
translation: 女子犯了什麼法？
a. 她超緊急用車輛的車。
b. 她超速。
c. 她違規停車。
d. 她沒有換新她的執照。
options: (A) She cut off an emergency vehicle.

questions.stem: What can we guess about the woman?
translation: 我們可以如何推測這名女子？
a. 她開一部老車。
b. 她對這名警察有好感。
c. 她破產了。
d. 她之前已經收到過很多罰單。
options: (B) She has a crush on the police officer.
(D) She has gotten many tickets before.

questions.stem: What might happen next?
translation: 接下來可能會怎樣？
a. 警察會道歉。
b. 女子被允許在此處停車。
c. 警察會移走這部車。
d. 女子會被逮捕。
options: (B) The woman will be allowed to park there.
(C) The police officer will move the car.

cards.passage:
W: Do you know where all the invoices from September are?
M: I put them right here in this cardboard box yesterday. They seem to have disappeared.
W: What? Do you know what this box is for?
M: Um…
W: Read the side of it.
M: It says, "Documents to be shredded."
W: Exactly.
M: So that means they've all been shredded?
W: Obviously.
M: I'm sorry, Doris.
W: I don't want any more apologies. Pack up your things. You're out of here.
cards.passageTranslation:
女： 你知道所有九月份的收據放在哪裡嗎？
男： 我昨天把它放在這裡的紙盒裡。看起來是不見了。
女： 什麼？你知道這個盒子是做什麼用的嗎？
男： 嗯…
女： 你唸一下旁邊的字。
男： 上面說，"待絞碎文件"。
女： 一點也沒錯。
男： 所以這意思是它已經全部都被絞碎了？
女： 顯然如此。
男： 我很抱歉，Doris。
女： 我不想再聽到任何道歉了。把你的東西收一收。你被開除了。

questions.stem: What happened to the September invoices?
translation: 九月份的收據怎麼了？
a. 男子把它們放進垃圾桶。
b. 男子把它們放進回收桶。
c. 被銷毀了。
d. 遺失了。
options: (A) The man put them in the trash bin.
(B) The man put them in the recycle bin.

questions.stem: What can we infer about the man?
translation: 關於男子我們可以推論什麼？
a. 他是新員工。
b. 他不喜歡這個女子。
c. 他之前已經犯過許多錯。
d. 他不太重視他的工作。
options: (C) He has made many mistakes before.
(D) He doesn't care much about his job.

questions.stem: What was the result of the conversation?
translation: 這段對話的結果為何？
a. 男子收到最後警告。
b. 男子被解僱。
c. 男子被獎勵。
d. 男子被交付新任務。
options: (A) The man received a final warning.
(D) The man was given a new task.

cards.passage:
W: When are you available to come have a look at the office space I mentioned?
M: This week my schedule is totally full. How about Monday morning next week?
W: It seems we have the opposite schedule. I've got several gaps in my schedule this week, but I am booked solid next week.
M: So what can we do? We don't want to delay this too long. We were hoping to start moving our things into the space as soon as possible.
W: Would you be willing to go have a look at the space yourself? I could leave the key with the guard downstairs.
M: Sure, why not?
W: Great. You already have the address, right?
M: Yes. Beside the Fort Mac Building, right?
W: No. It's a little tricky to find. Even though the address is on Main Street, the entrance to the building is not actually on Main, but around the corner on Delaware Ave and across the street from the Fort Mac building, just before the Ryan Air office.
M: Got it. Thanks for the tip.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：你什麼時候有空來看我提過的辦公室？
男：這週我的行程已滿。 下周一早上如何？
女：看來我們的行程表相反。我本週的行程有幾個空檔，但我下週滿檔。
男：那該怎麼辦？ 我們不想拖太久，我們希望盡快開始把東西搬進辦公室。
女：你願意自己去看嗎？ 我可以把鑰匙留給樓下的警衛。
男：當然可以啊。
女：太好了。 你已經有地址了對吧？
男：是的。 在福特邁可大廈旁邊，對吧？
女：不，有點不好找。 雖然地址是在主要街道上，實際大樓入口處不在那，是在特拉華大道的拐角處，福特邁可大廈對面，就在銳安航空辦公室之前。
男：知道了。 謝謝你的提醒。

questions.stem: When is the woman available?
translation: 該女士什麼時候有空？
a. 她本週有空，但下週沒有。
b. 她下週有空，但本週沒有。
c. 她本週和下週都有空。
d. 她本週或下週都沒空。
options: (A) She's available this week but not next week.
(B) She's available next week but not this week.
(C) She's available both this week and next week.
(D) She's available neither this week nor next week.

questions.stem: What is their solution to the problem of not being able to meet this week or next week?
translation: 以下選項是兩者對本周或下周都無法碰面的解決方案？
a. 他們會在兩星期後碰面。
b. 他們會在三星期後碰面。
c. 男子可以不在女子陪同下看地方。
d. 男子不需要在購買之前看地方。
options: (A) They will meet in two weeks.
(B) They will meet in three weeks.
(C) The man can go visit the property without the woman.
(D) The man doesn't need to visit the property before purchasing it.

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. Where is the entrance to the building?
translation: 請看圖表。大樓入口的位置在哪？
a. 位置 A
b. 位置 B
c. 位置 C
d. 位置 D

cards.passage:
W: Have you and your wife chosen the color for the bathroom yet?
M: We are going for an ocean theme, so we'd like blue.
W: Sure. Here is a palette showing all the blues we offer.
M: OK. Wow. It's kind of hard to decide.
W: Maybe you should wait and let your wife decide.
M: No, she's even more indecisive than me.
W: Let me give you some advice. You want to go as light as possible. All of these colors are going to look much darker when they are covering all the walls.
M: Good to know. I'll take the blue on the bottom of this row then.
cards.passageTranslation:
女：你和你太太選好浴室的顏色了嗎？
男：我們喜歡海洋主題，所以我們要藍色。
女：沒問題。這個調色板上有我們現有全部的藍色。
男：好。哇，有點難決定。
女：或許你應該等一下讓你的太太決定。
男：不，她甚至比我更優柔寡斷。
女：我來給你一些建議吧。你想要顏色盡可能淡的。當這些顏色塗滿全部的牆壁後，他們看起來會更暗。
男：多長一智。那我挑這一列最底部的藍色。

questions.stem: What is the woman's job?
translation: 女子的工作為何？
a. 室內設計師
b. 印刷店員工
c. 婚姻諮詢師
d. 建築工人

questions.stem: Why won't the man let his wife make the decision?
translation: 為什麼男子不讓他的妻子做決定？
a. 他不是個好丈夫。
b. 他太太不在意。
c. 他沒有太太。
d. 他太太不擅長做決定。
options: (D) His wife isn't good at making decisions.

questions.stem: Why does the woman recommend going lighter?
translation: 為什麼女子建議選淡色？
a. 比較便宜。
b. 比較容易。
c. 塗上牆後顏色看起來會更暗。
d. 看起來會更像真的海洋。
options: (C) It will look darker on the wall.
(D) It will look more like the real ocean.

cards.passage:
W: How's next Tuesday at three in the afternoon for you?
M: No can do. I've got an English class scheduled at that time.
W: Is it possible for you to postpone your class? This teleconference is quite important and can't be rescheduled yet again.
M: I guess so.
W: And think about this: the teleconference will be mostly in English, so you'll get some speaking and listening practice anyway.
M: But those Russians have such thick English accents. I can barely understand them.
W: Precisely. That's a true test of your ability!
cards.passageTranslation:
女：下禮拜三下午你可以嗎？
男： 沒辦法。我有一堂英文課已經排在那個時間了。
女： 你的課有可能可以延期嗎？這個電話會議十分重要，不能再改期了。
男： 我想是的。
女： 而且你想想看：這個電話會議會多半用英語進行，所以你至少可以練習一下說跟聽。
男： 可是這些俄羅斯人的英語有很重的口音。我幾乎聽不懂他們說什麼。
女： 一點也沒錯。那對你的能力是個真正的考驗！

questions.stem: What do we know about the teleconference?
translation: 關於這個電話會議我們知道些什麼？
a. 今天會舉行。
b. 他們會在男子的辦公室進行。
c. 大部分會以俄語進行。
d. 先前已經改過時間。
options: (A) It is going to take place today.
(B) They will hold it at the man's office.
(C) It will be conducted mostly in Russian.

questions.stem: Why doesn't the man think this will be good practice for his English?
translation: 為什麼男子不認為這會是他練習他的英語的好機會？
a. 他不會說太多話。
b. 很難聽得見其他與會者說話。
c. 要聽懂其他與會者的英語很難。
d. 他的英語老師更專業。
options: (B) It's hard to hear the other participants.
(C) It's difficult to understand the other participant's English.
(D) His English teacher is more professional.

questions.stem: What does the woman think about this?
translation: 女子對此有何想法？
a. 她認同。
b. 她認為這對男子會有幫助。
c. 她認為男子的英語不好。
d. 她認為俄羅斯人英語流利。
options: (B) She thinks it will be helpful for the man.
(C) She thinks the man's English isn't good.
(D) She thinks the Russians have good English.

cards.passage:
M: Anita, could you please change the CD.
W: Sure. Why?
M: This music is not really appropriate.
W: I actually chose this music because I thought it makes good background noise for shoppers.
M: I do like it. But head office says we can only play classical.
W: Seriously? How come?
M: According to them, people feel classier when they hear classical music, making them more willing to spend money.
W: Hm. I somehow doubt that's true.
cards.passageTranslation:
男：Anita，能不能請你換一下CD。
女：當然。怎麼了？
男：這個音樂並不是十分合適。
女：我選這個音樂是因為我以為它對購物的人來說是個好背景噪音。
男：我確實喜歡。可是總公司說我們只能播古典樂。
女：真的嗎？為什麼呢？
男：據他們的說法，當人們聽到古典樂時他們會覺得更高貴，使他們更願意花錢。
女：嗯。不知怎麼的，我懷疑那是真的。

questions.stem: What reason does the man give for making the woman change the music?
translation: 男子要女子更換音樂的理由為何？
a. 他不喜歡。
b. 他認為顧客不會喜歡。
c. 他認為當顧客聽見時會買得更多。
d. 那是來自他的上司的命令。
options: (B) He thinks customers won't like it.
(C) He thinks customers will buy more when they hear it.
(D) It said the order comes from above him.

questions.stem: Why might people spend more money when they hear certain kinds of music?
translation: 為什麼當人們聽見某幾種音樂時會花更多的錢？
a. 他們會覺得生氣，因此會想儘速離開商店。
b. 音樂會給人購物的活力。
c. 當他們聽到那種音樂會覺得更高貴。
d. 那只是種理論，原因不明。
options: (A) They will feel annoyed so they will want to leave the shop quickly.
(B) The music will give the energy for shopping.
(C) When they hear that kind of music, they will feel fancier.
(D) It's just a theory and the reasons are unknown.

questions.stem: What does the woman think about this idea?
translation: 女子對這個主意有什麼想法？
a. 她了解那是真的。
b. 她不相信。
c. 這個主意讓她很煩。
d. 她決定告訴總公司那不是真的。
options: (C) The idea bothers her a lot.
(D) She decides to tell head office it's not true.

cards.passage:
W: I'm pretty sure the supreme nachos were 11.99 on the menu. But here is says 13.99.
M: Oh, that's because you asked for extra sour cream, which is a two-dollar surcharge.
W: Oh! I forgot about that. And one more thing. I thought you guys only added a 10% service charge?
M: No, that was increased to 15% last March.
M2: Could we have that removed?
M: Was there something wrong with your service today?
M2: In fact, yes. Our appetizers took over 30 minutes and were overcooked. My steak was chewy, and my son never got the extra napkins that he asked for three times.
cards.passageTranslation:
女： 我很確定菜單上至尊玉米片是11.99元。可是這裡說是13.99元。
男： 喔，那是因為你加點了酸奶油，多收兩美金。
女： 喔！我忘了這點。還有一件事。我以為你們只加收一成服務費？
男： 不是，去年三月就調漲成15%了。
男2：我們可以刪除這部分嗎？
男： 今天的服務有什麼問題嗎？
男2： 其實是的。我們的開胃菜花了30分鐘才送來，而且煮過頭了。我的牛排很難嚼，還有我兒子要了三次額外的餐巾他都沒有拿到。

questions.stem: What was the first error on the bill?
translation: 帳單上第一個錯誤為何？
a. 服務生給他們收太多錢。
b. 服務生把他們沒點的東西算進去。
c. 服務生忘了把某樣東西算進去。
d. 其實並沒有錯誤。
options: (A) The waiter charged them too much.
(B) The waiter added an item they didn't order.
(C) The waiter forgot to include something.

questions.stem: How does the couple feel about the service charge?
translation: 這對夫婦對收取服務費這部分的感想如何？
a. 他們覺得合理。
b. 他們不覺得服務生應該收15%。
c. 他們不想付這筆錢，因為服務很差。
d. 他們希望可以降到一成。.
options: (B) They don't think waiters should get 15%.
(C) They don't want to pay it because the service was poor.

questions.stem: Which complaint is not mentioned?
translation: 哪一個抱怨沒有被提及？
a. 甜點讓人提不起胃口。
b. 開胃菜上得很慢。
c. 食物煮得不好。
d. 服務生丟三落四。
options: (C) The food was not well prepared.

cards.passage:
W: Corban, is the blueprint for the proposed wing ready yet?
M: Not quite. I still need to make a few adjustments.
W: But you told me it was basically finished last week.
M: True, but in this morning's meeting with the board of directors, I was informed that they want closets in all of the patient's rooms. I have to make several changes as a result.
W: Sorry, I wasn't aware of that. So how much more time do you need?
M: Two…three…no, make it four hours.
cards.passageTranslation:
女： Corban，那個廂房提案的設計圖準備好了嗎？
男： 不完全好。我還需要做些微調整。
女： 可是你上禮拜告訴我它基本上完成了。
男： 沒錯，但是在今天早上的董事會，我被告知說他們想要所有的病房內都有衣櫥。因此我得做幾個變更。
女： 抱歉，我不知道這件事。那麼你還需要多多少時間？
男： 兩…三…不，算四個小時好了。

questions.stem: Why is it taking Corban longer to finish the blueprint?
translation: 為什麼Corban要花更久的時間才能完成設計圖？
a. 他犯了一些錯誤。
b. 他的經理否決了第一個版本。
c. 有一些最後的變更要求。
d. 它比他原本想像的還要困難。
options: (B) His manager rejected the first version.
(D) It's more difficult than he thought it would be.

questions.stem: What is the blueprint for?
translation: 這是什麼的設計圖？
a. 醫院
b. 學校
c. 社區活動中心
d. 垃圾場

questions.stem: In how long does he think he will finish it?
translation: 他認為他多久可以完成？
a. 兩小時
b. 三小時
c. 四小時
d. 超過四小時

cards.passage: Everyone, if I could have your attention please, we will go ahead and get started. First on the agenda, next Friday is our parent-teacher meetings. I'll need everyone to have all of your grades entered for this semester and uploaded onto the school's webpage by this afternoon. If a student has an overall grade that is below 70, it will be mandatory that their parents come in for a meeting. Secondly, try to keep each meeting under 30 minutes because some parents will need to see multiple teachers. Lets really try to keep things moving as swiftly as possible. Lastly, if anyone can stay later in the evenings this week to help set up sign in sheets and refreshment tables, that would be great.
cards.passageTranslation: 大家好，請注意一下，我們要開始進行了。議程上的第一件事，下個禮拜五是我們的親師座談會。我需要大家在今天下午以前將本學期所有的成績輸入完畢並上傳到學校的網站上。如果有學生的總分在七十分以下，他們的家長就一定要到校與會。其次，盡量把每次面談時間控制在三十分鐘內，因為有些家長須和多位老師碰面。讓我們盡一切可能讓事情迅速進行。最後，如果有人這禮拜晚間可以留晚一點幫忙把簽到表跟點心桌準備好的話，那就太棒了。
vocabulary: discipline v. 懲處 as … as possible盡可能；愈…愈好
ex. We will investigate the bank robbery as soon as possible. 我們會盡快調查這樁搶案。
ex. Management has made a new rule that we all have to sign in when we get to work. 管理處的新規定要求我們去工作時都要簽到。
ex. The president of the Legislative Yuan was expelled from the party. 立法院長被開除黨籍。

questions.stem: Who are the likely listeners?
translation: 以下何人誰可能是聽眾？
a. 教職員
b. 家長
c. 學生
d. 校長

questions.stem: What would happen if a student got under 70 in all of his or her classes?
translation: 如果有學生的課堂總分低於七十分會怎樣？
a. 他/她就得要留級。
b. 他/她的家長就得到學校來參加面談。
c. 他/她會被學校開除。
d. 他/她的家長就得簽許多表格。
options: (A) He or she would have to repeat the grade.
(B) His or her parents will have to come in for many meetings.
(C) He or she would get expelled from the school.
(D) His or her parents will have to sign a lot of forms.

questions.stem: What can we infer about the upcoming event?
translation: 我們可以為這場即將到來的活動做什麼推論？
a. 學生將必須參加測驗。
b. 家長將必須登記新的課程。
c. 學生會被懲處。
d. 將會在學校裡舉行。
options: (A) Students will have to take an exam.
(B) Parents will need to register for new classes.
(D) It will take place at the school.

cards.passage: Tired of waking up with back pain? Sick of tossing and turning at night? Well, here at Mattress Emporium we guarantee that we can find the correct mattress for you. Due to an increase in inventory, we are slashing prices. Take the Sleep-O-Matic mattress with a retail price of $999 for example; we'll drop that down to $599. Families, are you in need of a new set of beds? Look no further because here at Mattress Emporium we are offering a family discount! If you buy one mattress, we will sell you a second mattress of any size at half price. Put an end to all of those agonizing sleepless nights and come on down to Mattress Emporium today. We're located two miles south of Highway 72, just past Taxidermy R' Us. Come see us today!
cards.passageTranslation:
受夠了起床時會背痛嗎？厭倦了夜裡輾轉難眠嗎？在「床墊王專賣店」這裡，我們保證你可以找到適合床墊。由於存貨量的增加，我們正在大降價。以「真好眠」這款零售價為999美金的床墊為例，我們會往下降到599美金。您家裡的床都需要整組換新嗎？別再找了，因為在「床墊王專賣店」這裡我們提供家庭優惠價！如果您買一張床墊，第二張任何尺寸的床墊我們就以半價優惠。結束這些令人苦腦的失眠夜，今天就來「床墊王專賣店」看看。我們位在72號公路往南二英里處，剛過「真皮標本行」。今天就來找我們吧！
顧客收據
品項#1：好眠加大雙人床 $699
品項#2：好眠單人床墊 $650
總共：$1349
vocabulary: toss v. 翻來覆去；翻滾
agonizing adj. 令人痛苦難忍的
advertisement n. 廣告 due to 由於；因為
ex. Due to my father's current health condition, I'll be spending the night in the hospital with him . 由於父親目前的健康狀況，我今晚會在醫院陪他。
in need of 需要；急需
ex. The hospital is desperately in need of type A blood. 醫院急需A型血。
put an end to結束；終止
ex. The sudden earthquake put an end to our meeting. 突然來的地震使我們的會議終止了。

questions.stem: What kind of an announcement is this?
translation: 這是一則什麼樣的通知？
a. 一則有教育性的演講
b. 一則廣告
c. 一則新聞報告
d. 一則語音訊息

questions.stem: Look at the graphic. How much will the second item cost once the sale discussed in the talk is applied?
translation: 請見圖表。談話中討論的售價若成交了，第二個品項的價格會是多少？

questions.stem: What is NOT true according to the announcement?
translation: 根據這則通告，以下何者為非？
a. 「床墊王專賣店」位在72號公路上。
b. 他們提供家庭特惠價。
c. 「真好眠」床墊特價是999美金。
d. 他們的存貨變多了。
options: (A) Mattress Emporium is located on Highway 72.

cards.passage: In the ongoing struggle to contain the Ebola virus, sources have confirmed that an infected French citizen is currently being treated for the disease in a hospital in Paris. The man, Pierre Tableau, 43, came to the Parisian hospital last Tuesday after experiencing symptoms of Ebola. Sources later confirmed that Tableau, a practicing nurse, had recently traveled to Liberia to visit distant relatives and to help volunteer in the understaffed hospitals of the nation's capital, Monrovia. When questioned, Liberian officials declared that upon departure, Tableau never mentioned that he had come in contact with anyone that he knew to be infected with the virus. Medical staff in Paris has quarantined Tableau in the east wing of a hospital in Paris. Friends and family close to Tableau are also being closely monitored to make sure the disease is contained.
cards.passageTranslation: 在這場持續圍堵伊波拉病毒的奮戰中，消息來源已經證實一名染有該病的法國公民目前正在巴黎一所醫院接受治療。四十三歲的男子Pierre Tableau，出現伊波拉感染的症狀後於上周二來到巴黎一所醫院。稍後消息證實Tableau是一名護士，最近曾前往賴比瑞亞探視遠親，並在首都蒙羅維亞內幾間人力不足的醫院內擔任志工。賴比瑞亞的海關人員聲稱Tableau離境接受詢問時，從未提到他曾與任何他知道染有該病毒的人有過接觸。巴黎的醫療人員已經將Tableau隔離在巴黎一所醫院內的東側。與Tableau親近的朋友和家人也在密切的監控下以確保疫情受到控制。
vocabulary: ongoing adj. 進行的；不間斷的
Ebola virus n. 伊波拉病毒
practicing adj. 在從事工作的
understaffed adj. 人員配備不足的
quarantine v. 使隔離 in contact with 與…接觸
ex. She was in contact with infected animals just before she started feeling unwell. 正好在她不舒服之前，她有接觸過被感染的動物。

questions.stem: Where can we infer that the man contracted Ebola?
translation: 我們可以推論這名男子是在何處染上伊波拉？
a. 法國
b. 賴比瑞亞
c. 巴黎
d. 歐洲

questions.stem: What is Mr. Tableau's profession?
translation: Tableau先生的職業是什麼？
a. 醫生
b. 新聞記者
c. 護士
d. 老師

questions.stem: What type of an announcement is this?
translation: 這是一則什麼類型的聲明稿？
a. 新聞報導
b. 商業廣告
c. 氣象報告
d. 路況通告

cards.passage: Before I get started, I just want to thank you all for coming tonight to celebrate Gary Harken's retirement. Over the past twenty years Gary has been like a father to me here at Benson & Hedges. When I first joined the sales staff in 1994, I was one of four salesmen under Gary. In three short years, he tripled our staff and our regional coverage. But working with Gary was more than just business. Sure, he was a great boss, but he was an even better friend. Gary showed me that treating people with respect and compassion was the most import aspect of sales, no matter how ruthless the market can be. So this is how we will remember you, Gary: you were a nice guy that finished first. I truly thank you and wish you the best of luck.
cards.passageTranslation: 開始以前，我想謝謝你們大家前來慶祝Gary Harken的退休。過去二十年在Benson & Hedges，Gary對我來說就像個父親一樣。1994年我開始踏進業務部門，我是Gary底下的四個業務員之一。短短三年內，他將業務人員的數量以及業務涵蓋區域增加了三倍。我和Gary不只是共事而已。的確，他是個很棒的上司，但他更是一位好朋友。Gary教我要誠以待人以及同理心是推銷技巧中最重要的一門，不管市場是多麼的無情。Gary，在我們的心目中：你永遠是第一名的好夥伴。我要誠摯的感謝你並祝福你一切順利。
vocabulary: ruthless adj. 無情的；殘忍的
gratitude n. 感激之情；感謝 no matter how 不管怎樣
ex. No matter how hard I try to please my boss, she's never satisfied with my work. 不管我怎樣試著讓我的老闆高興，她永遠對我的工作不滿意。

questions.stem: What can we infer about the speaker?
translation: 關於演說者我們可以做以下何者推論？
a. 他是Gary的兒子。
b. 他最近失業了。
c. 他即將要退休。
d. 他對Gary深感敬佩。

questions.stem: What was Gary's relationship to the speaker?
translation: Gary與演說者是以下何種關係？
a. Gary曾經是他的上司。
b. Gary曾經是他的部屬。
c. Gary曾經是他的競爭對手。
d. Gary曾經是他的客戶。

questions.stem: What is the purpose of this talk?
translation: 這段談話的用意為何？
a. 歡迎新同事加入
b. 在某位同事退休時對他表示感謝
c. 抱怨某位同事的工作表現
d. 通知同事有派對即將舉行
options: (A) to welcome a new colleague to the workplace
(B) to show gratitude to a colleague as they retire
(C) to complain about a colleague's performance
(D) to inform colleagues about an upcoming party

cards.passage: Ladies and gentlemen, as we approach the departure terminals, please check your ticket to confirm which terminal your flight will be departing from. As a friendly reminder, Domestic flights on Mountain West Airways, Beta, Condor Airlines, and Budget Zone will depart from terminal one.
